programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: The crime-busting cleric tries to prove a businessman has been framed for murder, but incriminating evidence forces him to reconsider the wisdom of his crusade. Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson star

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: A man accused of murder tries to get himself off the hook by claiming to be Frank's long-lost son. Starring Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is befriended by a man who has family troubles and repays a favour

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Murder occurs with explosive violence as a woman is active in the fight for control of supposedly oil-rich land intended for a housing subdivision

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part one of two. Walker learns the truth behind the legend of Texas Ranger Hayes Cooper when he discovers an ancient journal. Adventure, starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: When a celebrity photographer is found dead in his car, Horatio and the team rule out the possibility of it being an accident, as all the evidence points to the involvement of a Hollywood star captured in a compromising position by the paparazzo. Drama, starring David Caruso and Emily Procter

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: CIA agent Trent Kort returns to track down dangerous criminal Jonathan Siravo and Gibbs is forced to put his personal feelings aside to assist in the investigation. The duo manage to get hold of Siravo's accountant, who agrees to reveal all he knows in exchange for full immunity, but the trail leads to an old man in a coma in what seems to be a case of stolen identity

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Brandon Valdivia falls to his death from a rooftop bar and is initially thought to have committed suicide, but his family refuse to accept that possibility and persuade a congressman to put some pressure on the admiral. Sam and Callen are then brought in to re-evaluate the case and discover that Valdivia may have been involved in money laundering with a fellow marine.

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble performs a heroic act and it all but costs him his life

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: History seems to be repeating itself when a student is charged with poisoning a man in the same town his father killed someone in years ago

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony and Ziva track a suspected terrorist to a warehouse, where they find traces of radioactive metal - together with evidence suggesting a nuclear attack is being planned on Washington, DC. Unfortunately, news of the threat leaks out to the public, triggering widespread panic. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part two. Hayes Cooper joins the ranks of the Texas Rangers when he sets out to avenge the deaths of his friends. Starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A woman is shot dead in an apartment while keeping watch on a man on a yacht in a marina, and the killer takes her camera and computer. A flash drive is recovered from the victim's baseball cap and surveillance footage on it includes images of Callen, who deduces that the pictures were taken in the days leading up to the attempt on his life and that the dead woman was the last person to see him before he was shot

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Iraq war veteran Damon Werth asks Gibbs and the team to investigate the death of another former marine, who was murdered while working as a lorry driver, so Ziva hits the road to catch the killer. Joe Spano and Paul Telfer guest star

programme.desc: When a young mother is found badly beaten in an alleyway, Sun Hill CID is drawn into her tale of debt, blackmail and a complex love life. Christopher Timothy and Duncan James guest star

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Oliver eagerly anticipates the award ceremony, where he and Jac may be honoured for their research. However, the arrival of a patient with an unusual condition forces him to choose between his moment of glory and carrying out an operation. Chantelle thinks Lleucu has ruined her Valentine's Day plans, while Sacha discovers he may not be able to give Chrissie everything he had intended

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Dixie is upset when Cyd confronts her about recommending Greg for a transfer and considers resigning herself. Jane Hazlegrove and Ben Price star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A couple return from a night away to discover their baby has been abducted from her bed. But as Tony gently interrogates the parents, another suspect emerges

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Terry moves into the Vic. Dixon confesses his affair with Jamie to George.

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah leads the charge to rescue James, whose ordeal continues when his sexuality becomes known, and puts her relationship with George at risk when a disturbing discovery forces her to reveal her darkest secret

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan appears to have an ulterior motive for taking an interest in Tricky Woo's diet, and Mrs Pumphrey gets James to be the judge at the vegetable show. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Alison decides she can manage on her own and rents a remote country cottage, but the discovery that there's an arsonist at large soon has Paul fearing for her safety. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant, Tamzin Malleson and Tim Vincent

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon is convinced her 30th birthday is just the start of another three decades of misery, prompting Tracey to question the wisdom of throwing a party. Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson star

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: A tattered shoebox filled with money arrives for Jeffrey, with instructions from Joe Maplin that it be used to bribe local councillors to not build a hospital next to the holiday camp. Comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth attends an exclusive auction, while Daisy and Rose try to cool the ardour of a woman who seems determined to marry Daddy. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Judy Cornwell and Josephine Tewson

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard employs Clegg to take care of several suspect picnic objects, but one item receives his refusal and Howard will not look after it until Pearl is out of sight. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Robert Fyfe

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: Jane and Davey investigate a skydiving accident and discover that someone had tampered with the victim's parachute. Crime drama, starring Claire Goose

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: A twisted fetishist is murdering women in a horrific echo of an unsolved case from the 1980s. Luther is keen that he and his team concentrate all their resources into catching the copycat before he or she strikes again, only for his focus to be divided by a second case involving a malicious internet tormentor found dead in his home. Pulled in every direction, the cop's patience begins to wear thin, so the last thing he needs is to deal with a road accident - until he sets eyes on one of the women involved. Is he finally on the verge of finding love? Crime drama, starring Idris Elba, cwith Warren Brown, Sienna Guillory, Nikki Amuka-Bird and Dermot Crowley

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Brian finds the library card of cold case victim Dr Richard Symes, leading the team to reinvestigate the death of the professor, who perished after falling from the roof of his college three years' previously. Sandra and Gerry interview Symes' widow, who believes principal Jeremy Ventham drove her husband to suicide following a conflict over teaching methods. Crime drama, starring Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: DI Pat North is assigned to look into a case that may clear a man convicted of murder eight years ago, and finds that DS Walker was in charge of the original inquiry. Drama, starring Kate Buffery, David Hayman and James Wilby

programme.title: The Casual Vacancy
programme.desc: Drama adapted from the novel by JK Rowling, set in an English village whose seemingly idyllic facade hides a community at war. At the parish council meeting, chairman Howard Mollison introduces his plans to turn the community centre into a health spa, but he reckons without local solicitor Barry Fairbrother, who makes an impassioned speech about the importance of the centre to the impoverished people of the nearby estate. However, an unexpected turn of events leaves an empty seat on the council - leading to the biggest battle Pagford has ever seen. Michael Gambon, Rory Kinnear, Keeley Hawes and Julia McKenzie star

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of a bookies' clerk leads the team to 29-year-old Vic Tyler - who Sam is shocked to discover is his own father. He realises the time when he and his mother were abandoned is coming up and desperately tries to persuade the small-time gambler not to leave his wife and child. Meanwhile, Gene sees an opportunity to bring down a crime syndicate

programme.desc: Tony wonders what to do with the photo of Dixon and Jamie. Pauline has a phone call from a woman called Maggie in Ireland saying she's her sister

programme.desc: Councillor Dixon's sex life is exposed by his wife. Simon and Irene come to blows over Tony's sexuality. Carol is threatened over Billy's trial

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan heads for trouble after castrating a pig and forgetting to dispose of the 'bits', and James provides some advice for a would-be bridegroom. Guest starring Thora Hird and Warren Clarke, with Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: The body of a woman is recovered from a reservoir and police suspect she may have died as a result of a lovers' suicide pact - but the post-mortem result triggers a full-scale murder inquiry. Meanwhile, Dangerfield and his pregnant daughter are drawn closer together. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Darryl plots to spend a night with Tracey by escaping from hospital, while Sharon makes her own plans for the evening's entertainment. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.desc: Ted gets up to his usual tricks, using every money-raising fiddle in the book to fend off bailiffs threatening him with prison for failing to pay maintenance to his ex-wife. Paul Shane and Simon Cadell star in Jimmy Perry and David Croft's holiday camp comedy

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth cannot suppress her excitement when she hears Emmet is rehearsing an amateur production of The Boyfriend, and resolves to land herself a part in the show. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and David Griffin

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley and Barry devise another money-making plan to sell hot dogs to weary hikers and travellers, while Clegg, Truly and Tom fondly remember Compo and imagine how Nora Batty's arrival in Australia will cause misery for the folks Down Under. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: An Inspector Calls
programme.desc: Adaptation of JB Priestley's stage play. In 1912, a detective (David Thewlis) arrives in a town where a young woman has committed suicide. Interrupting a wealthy family in the midst of a lavish party, he unravels the events that led to the woman's death - an investigation with devastating repercussions as secrets are exposed and each member of the family realises they have all unwittingly played a part. Also starring Miranda Richardson and Ken Stott

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: After being released from jail, notorious paedophile John Davies admits to the abduction and murder of a child 25 years previously. Suspicious of his motives, the detectives reopen the unsolved case in an unusual attempt to prove his innocence, reinvestigating the boy's disappearance while on a demonstration with his left-wing activist parents. Crime drama, guest starring Adrian Schiller and Samantha Bond

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: New eye-witness testimony and a television documentary about the McCready case casts doubt on the original murder investigation. DS Walker is suspended from duty and decides to return to Glasgow to find new evidence to prove his innocence, in order to save his career, relationship and reputation

programme.title: The Casual Vacancy
programme.desc: As the parish council election approaches, the townsfolk are on tenterhooks, awaiting the next post from 'the Ghost of Barry Fairbrother'. Who is behind the internet messages and what will they say next? In an attempt to smooth things over, Samantha throws a dinner party for all the warring factions, but when Howard and Shirley unexpectedly arrive things quickly spiral out of control. Across in the Fields, Terri seems to be over her addiction, giving Krystal hope of a new future for her family. Drama adapted from the novel by JK Rowling, starring Michael Gambon, Keeley Hawes, Abigail Lawrie and Keeley Forsyth

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Police drama, starring John Simm. Sam experiences visions that seem to suggest a return to the 21st century, but wakes to find himself still stuck in 1973. His mood lifts when he comes face to face with the younger version of a killer he arrested in 2006 and realises he has the chance to prevent a crime before it happens - but his unorthodox methods surprise colleagues at the station. With Philip Glenister and Marc Warren

programme.desc: Tess and Nadia reluctantly make their way to the party together, only to be confronted by the sight of a burning man in the road. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. When a talented young athlete is found battered to death in a park, officers interrogate fellow guests at a house party he had attended earlier that evening. programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Carol and Alan get a visit from the police who say they know the gang who threatened Carol. They want Carol to identify the gang at the police station

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Huw and Robbie are smitten with Mary. Irene and Terry flirt. Carol and Alan are introduced to their Victim Liaison Officer. Diane gets a supermarket job

programme.desc: Elizabeth decides to organise a formal party to demonstrate how out of her depth Sarah is. Meanwhile, Carolyn is shocked by Jack's revelation when she challenges him to confront his intimacy issues, and Regina returns home from Europe with news about Sarah. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: The Jersey detective is shocked when he returns from sick leave to find his job at stake, and a colleague is murdered when investigating international gun sales. Crime drama, starring John Nettles

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: The doctors discover a bogus GP has been making house calls to Paul's patients under his name and call in police to investigate. The medic worries his reputation could be tarnished permanently - especially as the imposter seems to know his every move. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey invites an old school friend to stay for a few days, leaving Sharon out in the cold. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Quite a few feathers are ruffled as Joe Maplin pays a secret visit to the camp and decides Gladys should be promoted to head Yellowcoat - prompting her to sew on some stripes to display her superior rank. With Ruth Madoc, Simon Cadell, Su Pollard and Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Although Emmet's musical is already fully cast, Hyacinth remains convinced that his shyness is the only reason she has not been given a starring role. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, David Griffin and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley demonstrates his new hand-crafted trolley, complete with deluxe car seat - guaranteed to bring back childhood memories to the dispirited Smiler. Meanwhile, Barry gets into a tangle when he takes up yoga. Gordon Wharmby, Stephen Lewis and Mike Grady star

programme.title: Inspector Alleyn Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Celebrity portrait painter Agatha Troy asks Alleyn to investigate the death of a venerable Shakespearean actor whose portrait she had been commissioned to paint. Drama, starring Patrick Malahide, with Belinda Lang

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A recent rape is linked to two similar attacks at a chocolate factory 10 years previously, leaving the team with no choice but to reopen all three cases. The detectives uncover news not only of an unhappy workforce, but also of an alleged suicide, and their investigation becomes even more complicated when they discover a severed finger was found in one of the company's snacks. After a visit to the doctor, Gerry decides to quit smoking, and instead of resorting to nicotine patches or gum, he enlists Sandra to help him quit

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Part one of two. Walker is assigned to investigate the discovery of a young woman's skeleton, found underneath the patio of what used to be a boarding house, but is paired with Pat's ex-boyfriend Jeff. Drama from Widows creator Lynda LaPlante, starring David Hayman and Paul Kynman

programme.title: The Casual Vacancy
programme.desc: Tensions rise in Pagford as the parish council election approaches and each side steps up its campaign, with the fate of Sweetlove House hanging in the balance. Meanwhile, Miles pines for a quiet life and promises Samantha that once the vote is over everything will calm down, but she doesn't believe him, and Howard's dark secret is revealed in the Ghost's final post. Krystal returns home to find Terri back to her old ways, Hope seems more fragile than ever and news of a tragedy leaves the whole community reeling

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam is shocked when his mentor from 2006 arrives at the station, and offers his advice when the new recruit becomes the butt of Ray's racist jokes. programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the opening day from the All England Club, as the first round continues in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. There was a major upset on Centre Court on day one when the tournament was last held in 2019, with Yulia Putintseva defeating second seed Naomi Osaka 7-6, 6-2. This was followed by a straight-sets victory for home favourite Kyle Edmund over Jaume Munar, and the London resident will hope the support of the fans can help him to go far this time around. Elsewhere, Karolina Pliskova followed up her triumph at Eastbourne with a 6-2, 7-6 victory over Zhu Lin, and Coco Gauff demonstrated her enormous potential when she prevailed 6-4, 6-4 against five-time champion Venus Williams at the age of just 15

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bernie tells Tiffany the weight-loss pills are not working fast enough and she needs stronger ones but her friend refuses. Vi encourages Stuart and Rainie to find a distraction from Abi, prompting them to make a decision about the surrogacy. Rocky has an idea to bring Mila and Iqra back together, while Isaac tells Lola he has a surprise for her

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany reluctantly gives more pills to Bernie, who is disappointed to discover she has not lost as much weight as she had hoped - convincing her she has blown her chance of becoming Rainie's surrogate. Isaac pays a visit to Ruby at the club - and he has plenty to say - and Kathy helps to clear the air between Mila and Iqra

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Lucky tries to overcome her personal crisis by throwing herself into work. But in her eagerness to go above and beyond she oversteps the mark. A patient accuses Dominic of leaving an unwanted item in his body during an operation, and Kylie put her foot in it with her flatmates

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design an Ibiza-themed party garden for a family in Wokingham, Berkshire. All concerned hope to come up with some cool ideas to transform this unloved plot into a laid-back Balearic chill out lounge on a budget of £3,000

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Olivia Colman finds some surprises in her ancestral history, and her belief that most of those in her family tree emanate from Norfolk turns out to be far from the truth. Olivia is astonished to discover that she needs to travel to India to find out more about her great-great-great grandmother Harriot, who was an orphan in 1811 on a ship bound for England from Calcutta. Harriot's father was an an Englishman, but the identity of her mother, who gave birth to her in a remote Indian village, is a mystery Olivia delights in solving

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Grime artist Saskilla concludes the series with a look at ongoing issues with diversity in the music industry, questioning the long-term benefits of the Blackout Tuesday social media campaign and if the money pledged by major labels is enough to bring about lasting change

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the fifth and sixth last-16 matches, which were Croatia v Spain at Parken in Copenhagen and France v Switzerland at National Arena Bucharest

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Citizen Khan star Adil Ray hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Conservative MP Nicky Morgan joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: France v Switzerland. A chance to see the sixth match in the last 16, which took place at National Arena Bucharest

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development potential of a Warwickshire property above a former brewery, a Staffordshire house with a huge back garden and a former farm building in Nottinghamshire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate a lethal shooting from 1937 in which a barge captain was allegedly murdered by his best friend

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: At the UK's most remote hospital, the Gilbert Bain, expectant mum Kirsti prepares to give birth by C-section with the help of the theatre team

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and Nick Hall provide advice to two teams competing at Nottingham racecourse. Presented by Charlie Ross

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the second day from the All England Club, as the first round continues in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. Roger Federer began his bid for a record-extending ninth Wimbledon title at this stage in 2019 and surprisingly lost the first set to Lloyd Harris, before turning on the style to prevail 3-6, 6-1, 6-2, 6-2. Another Wimbledon great was next up on Centre Court, with Serena Williams winning 6-2, 7-5 against qualifier Giulia Gatto-Monticone, before fourth seed Kiki Bertens secured a comfortable 6-3, 6-2 victory over Mandy Minella

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: England v Germany (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the seventh and penultimate last-16 match, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. This mouthwatering clash will bring back all sorts of memories for both nations of their previous high-profile meetings, and England manager Gareth Southgate will also be keen to lead his side to victory, having famously missed in the penalty shoot-out against Germany in the Euro 96 semi-final. England's 1-0 win over Czech Republic in their final group match helped them to first place in Group D without conceding a goal, despite not playing at their best, but they will still be confident against a German side that shipped five goals in their fixtures against France, Portugal and Hungary

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Sweden v Ukraine (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the eighth and final last-16 fixture, as the quarter-final line-up is decided at Hampden Park in Glasgow. The Swedes topped Group E after drawing with pre-tournament pool favourites Spain in their opener, before overcoming Slovakia 1-0 and then edging a pulsating encounter with Poland 3-2 thanks to Emil Forsberg's second and third goals of the tournament and an injury-time winner courtesy of Viktor Claesson. Ukraine seemed set for an early exit, having lost to the Netherlands and Austria, but other results went their way and they were the only third-placed side to progress with just three points

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne offer their own unique take on the tournament, reacting to the final last-16 matches and looking forward to the quarter-finals, with celebrity guests and a few surprises

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Dylan's search for Emma's mother turns up troublesome results, and he is forced to confront the difficult truth of his findings. An unexpected discovery brings Norman home. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma finds herself torn between Romero and her son, while Norman realises just how much everyone has changed in his absence. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see one of the final two last-16 matches - either England v Germany at Wembley Stadium or Sweden v Ukraine at Hampden Park

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the stories of a quirky property in Fulham, a huge renovation in Tamworth, Staffordshire, and a terrace in Bradford

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the third day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, featuring the opening second-round action from the outside courts

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents further coverage of the third day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, featuring more second-round matches. There was heartbreak for British number one Kyle Edmund at this stage two years ago when he was beaten in five sets by experienced Spaniard Fernando Verdasco. That match was followed on Centre Court by a comfortable 6-3, 6-2, 6-2 victory for eventual champion Novak Djokovic over Denis Kudla

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public. Here they bid for an unusual item of hairdressing memorabilia, something salvaged from a tip, a classic piece of industrial furniture and a beloved toy

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage musical instrument expert Pete Woods takes on a tarnished, dented saxophone that once played to lift people's spirits during the Blitz, but has not made a sound in more than 40 years. Silversmith Brenton West's skills are tested when he is handed an 80-year-old brass kettle in desperate need of professional attention. Saddler Suzie Fletcher restores a leather shaving mirror used by troops in the trenches of the First World War, and metal-worker Dominic Chinea sharpens up his act with a vintage carpenter's fret saw that has seen better days

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Bad Influencer: The Great Insta Con
programme.desc: The rise and fall of internet wellness guru Belle Gibson, who cultivated an adoring online following with the story that she had successfully treated her own cancer by eating a plant-based diet and using alternative natural therapies. There was one major problem with Belle's story - she never had cancer

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. First shown as Henry's home country prepared to host Euro 2016 following November 2015's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross looks at buying and selling design classics of the past, looking back at some of the best buys from past programmes. As well as revisiting some of the show's favourite experts and auctioneers, Charlie recalls a visit to the Brighton Pavilion where he learned all about its connection with a famous hat designer

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants John, Maria, Caroline and Mark vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Optics expert Richard Biggs works on a home-made telescope, built by the owner's grandfather from bits of scrap during the Second World War. Saddler Suzie Fletcher is brought a leather briefcase once owned by an intrepid traveller, while furniture restorer Will Kirk works his magic on a vintage writing bureau. The final challenge sees Dominic Chinea reviving a rusty child's seesaw that dates from the 1930s and was ahead of its time - not only did it go up and down, but it also spun round. Can Dominic get it spinning again?

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the opening day from the All England Club, featuring the first round of the men's and ladies' singles competitions, including Novak Djokovic v Jack Draper. The tournament was cancelled last year for the first time since the Second World War due to the Covid-19 pandemic, but it is expected to be business as usual this time around. As the defending men's champion, Djokovic will get proceedings under way on Centre Court, as he did in 2019, when he recorded a 6-3, 7-5, 6-3 victory over Philipp Kohlschreiber. The Serbian will be entering this competition on a high after recently being crowned French Open champion, ending the dominance of Rafael Nadal with a stunning four-set victory in the semi-finals, before coming from two sets down to defeat Stefanos Tsitsipas in the final, and it would be one of the biggest upsets in Wimbledon history should he fail at the first hurdle against British wild card Draper. After coming so close to his maiden Grand Slam title,

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents highlights of the opening day's play at the All England Club, where the first round got under way in the men's and ladies' singles. Novak Djokovic was scheduled on Centre Court to begin the defence of the title he won when the tournament was last held in 2019

programme.title: Victoria Wood: The Secret List
programme.desc: With a career spanning more than 40 years and crossing every possible genre, Victoria Wood amassed a back catalogue bulging with sublime comedy moments. The first of two documentaries offers a unique opportunity to see the very best bits of Victoria's work that she loved the most, drawing on some of the 20 sketches chosen by her in a notebook discovered after her death. Plus, contributors including Russell T Davies, Ken Loach, Winsome Pinnock, Jane Wymark and Joan Armatrading discuss Victoria's broad catalogue of award-winning comedy. Narrated by Sara Pascoe

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Glastonbury in the 90s
programme.desc: The story of Glastonbury in the 1990s, arguably the festival's redefining decade. It was the era television cameras came, dance music conquered and the festival changed beyond all recognition. Told through archival material from the time and by those who were there, it's voiced by Skin from Skunk Anansie, who were the last headliners of the decade

programme.title: Glastonbury in the 21st Century
programme.desc: Dizzee Rascal narrates a look at the festival since the turn of the century, with appearances by emerging global headliners such as Coldplay and the Killers, and a wildly packed Chemical Brothers performance from 2000. Plus, the much-debated arrival of hip-hop with Jay-Z in 2008, Beyoncé's game-changing performance in 2011, Kylie Minogue's return and Stormzy's defining headline set in 2019, as well as the Sunday afternoon Legend slots

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents an archive edition Nature in Art in Gloucestershire, the world's first museum and art gallery dedicated to art inspired by nature. While picking up a few artistic tips, Ellie introduces a selection of stories from the archives that demonstrate how the countryside has been an abiding influence, from early man to modern-day artists braving the extremes to capture a landscape or using wildlife itself to create a picture

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: University Hospitals Coventry and Warwickshire NHS Trust assess the aftermath of the pandemic and its long-term impact, and with the plastic surgery department finally able to start elective operations again, difficult decisions must be made over the investment in robotic surgery

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement officers James and Mark help an 85-year-old man who is owed thousands by a roofing company who failed to pay the money the courts awarded

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine the case of an 18-year-old hotel employee who was accused of murdering a titled guest in 1922

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham and Carole Baxter are being neighbourly in Beechgrove as they swap stories over the garden fence between Brian's Alpine garden and Carole's seaside garden. Plus, a catch-up with Sophie McKilligan in her new role as gardener at Culzean Castle in Ayrshire

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As midsummer approaches, it's the perfect time for Monty Don to place succulents outside at Longmeadow, as well as planting out dahlias and tender vegetables. Carol Klein visits Trebah Garden in Cornwall, where she finds plants which can add early summer pazazz to borders. Nick Bailey is at RHS Wisley to look at a variety of shrubs that can be used as alternatives to traditional box hedging

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tulsen Tollett presents highlights and analysis from the latest round of matches in Super League. Champions St Helens have again been impressive in the early months of the season, but this week they faced a tricky trip to Hull Kingston Rovers, who themselves had been in good form so far this year. Catalans were away to Castleford, Wigan travelled to Wakefield and Salford were at home to Leeds. Strugglers Leigh Centurions were away to Warrington, and Hull FC played Huddersfield

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Kym Marsh presents an appeal on behalf of the Lullaby Trust, a charity that offers emotional and practical support to families who have lost a young child

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the second day from the All England Club, as the first round continues in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. Simona Halep won the women's singles title when the competition was last held in 2019, dominating seven-time champion Serena Williams in the final, so would normally get today's proceedings under way on Centre Court, as is tradition, but she has been ruled out of the tournament due to a calf injury. It was Angelique Kerber who started as champion two years ago and she prevailed 6-4, 6-3 against German compatriot Tatjana Maria. Over on the No. 1 Court, there were also straight-sets victories for top seed Ashleigh Barty, British number one Johanna Konta and two-time champion Rafael Nadal, against Zheng Saisai, Ana Bogdan and Yuichi Sugita respectively. On the outside courts, 2004 title-winner Maria Sharapova retired hurt when 5-0 down in the third set against Pauline Parmentier, double champion Petra Kvitova comfortably defeated Ons Jabeur,

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents highlights of the second day's play at the All England Club, where the first round continued in the men's and ladies' singles

programme.title: Victoria Wood: The Secret List
programme.desc: With a career spanning more than 40 years and crossing every possible genre, Victoria Wood amassed a back catalogue bulging with sublime comedy moments. This second of two programmes draws on further favourite sketches chosen by her in a notebook discovered after her death. Plus, contributors discuss her broad catalogue of award-winning comedy

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Sri Lanka. Action from the first contest in the three-match series, held at Emirates Riverside in Durham. This is the first England one-day match since their seven run loss to India at the end of March, and the series will be followed by another three one-day internationals against Pakistan

programme.title: The Statue Wars: One Summer in Bristol
programme.desc: On Sunday, June 7, 2020, protestors marching to support the Black Lives Matter movement tore down the statue of slave trader Edward Colston and threw it in Bristol's harbour. Caught in the eye of this storm was Bristol's mayor Marvin Rees, the first directly elected mayor of Black African heritage of a major European city. Born and bred in Bristol and himself a descendant of enslaved people, how would he hold the city together in the face of rising tensions that threaten to explode into violent confrontation?

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works at a veterinary practice in rural Cumbria, learning why the health of livestock is vital to keeping the country fed, helping to keep working animals in good condition and assisting the vets who provide round-the-clock care

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan hope to see the rare turtle dove at Wild Ken Hill in Norfolk, while Gillian Burke gets close to young Manx shearwaters in Northern Ireland and Iolo Williams ducks into the river at Alladale to look for lampreys

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate a lethal shooting from 1937 in which a barge captain was allegedly murdered by his best friend

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts the show from Norfolk's biggest Antique Centre in King's Lynn. Experts Caroline Hawley and Mark Stacey assist the Reds and the Blues as they do battle

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Theo Paphitis steps in for Peter Jones, joining regular Dragons Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies to assess more money-making schemes. A musical inventor shows off his drumming training device, and four children make a first aid demonstration to secure investment for their parents' business. Plus, pitches for culinary alcohol and a prototype shoe mechanism

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the third day from the All England Club, featuring second-round matches. Eventual women's champion Simona Halep prevailed 6-3, 4-6, 6-2 against Romanian compatriot Mihaela Buzarnescu at this stage two years ago, and then 15-year-old prodigy Coco Gauff impressed again with a 6-3, 6-3 victory over Magdalena Rybarikova. There was British disappointment as Heather Watson lost in straight sets to 20th seed Anett Kontaveit, and Wimbledon remained the only Grand Slam to elude Stan Wawrinka when he lost to Reilly Opelka in five sets. Other notable victors on day three in 2019 included Karolina Pliskova, Caroline Wozniacki, Kevin Anderson and Milos Raonic, who defeated Monica Puig, Veronika Kudermetova, Janko Tipsarevic and Robin Hasse respectively

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents highlights of the third day's play from the All England Club, where the men's and ladies' singles were due to continue with round two, as the players continued their bids to win the titles claimed by Novak Djokovic and Simona Halep when the tournament was last held in 2019

programme.title: Stonehenge: The Lost Circle Revealed
programme.desc: Using cutting-edge research, a dedicated team of archaeologists has compiled the evidence to fill in a 400-year gap in man's knowledge of the bluestones of Stonehenge. By combining innovative 3D scanning techniques, traditional field archaeology and novel laboratory analysis, the team has discovered where the stones were quarried and where they first stood. Professor Alice Roberts shows how the team made its discoveries and joins Professor Mike Parker Pearson to piece together the final parts of the puzzle, solving one of the toughest challenges ever faced by archaeologists

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: John McEnroe: Still Rockin' at 60
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents an intimate profile of the tennis champion. The programme features contributions from family and friends including his wife - new wave singer-songwriter Patty Smyth - long-time sporting rival Bjorn Borg, and Chrissie Hynde of the Pretenders, who provides an insight into McEnroe's musical passions. Sue pays a visit to McEnroe's art gallery and examines how he has continued playing until the age of 60 after his retirement from the professional circuit

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson, Brian Cunningham and George Anderson focus on people gardening in smaller spaces. Kirsty plants up a range of trendy containers with plants to attract pollinators, while George is growing quick crops in small spaces

programme.title: Inside Culture
programme.desc: Mary Beard hands over the reins to cultural historian Shahidha Bari to explore the importance of self-expression, from the clothes we wear to the art and music we make. Shahidha visits artist Tracey Emin in her home town of Margate, and asks if is there a cost to revealing so much in your art. In Dundee, Shahidha also visits the new V&A exhibition on nightclub design

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan investigate how British hedgehogs are coping this spring, and Megan McCubbin joins them live in Norfolk to discuss efforts to save the corncrake. Iolo Williams is in the Scottish Highlands, checking on some very special wildcats, and Gillian Burke has a fishy tale about flapper skates in Northern Ireland

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a faraway mountain nestled in misty clouds and foothills, creating the scene using soft, brilliant colours

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Champagne and caviar flow non-stop in the ship's casino, where a professional gambler plans to clean up and a couple of married croupiers aim to earn enough to buy a new home. Meanwhile, psychic songstress Jane tells fortunes all round

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed takes viewers on a remarkable journey to places rarely seen as she travels through Iran to tell the story of its complex and fascinating people, culture and history. She begins by visiting places rarely seen by Western audiences to reveal how the Persian empire was born, the mysterious writing of its early people and how its powerful kings built a vast realm that became the envy of the ancient world

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob speaks to David Hockney about his new work at Westminster Abbey - a towering stained-glass window commissioned to celebrate 65 years since Queen Elizabeth II's coronation there. The artist famously refused a knighthood and declined an invitation to paint the Queen's portrait, so the announcement came as something of a surprising move from an artist whose career has continually defied expectation

programme.title: Face to Face
programme.desc: An archive interview with artist David Hockney

programme.title: Bob Ross: The Happy Painter
programme.desc: Documentary taking a behind-the-scenes look at American painter Bob Ross's journey from humble beginnings to pop culture icon, charting how one local TV commercial evolved into a viral phenomenon that continues to inspire millions around the world. The film reveals the public and private sides of Bob Ross through accounts from close friends and family, childhood photographs and rare archive footage. Interviewees recount his gentle, mild-mannered demeanour and unwavering dedication to wildlife as well as disclosing little-known facts about his famous curly hair - which was naturally straight - and his fascination with fast cars

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Comedy about Julia, a middle-class mother who moves out of her comfort zone and reluctantly invites the entire class to her kid's birthday party. With her mum still refusing to help out, she relies on Liz's party hacks and Kevin's entertaining skills. Starring Anna Maxwell-Martin and Lucy Punch

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: A school fundraiser is not Julia's idea of an evening out but somehow she finds herself heavily involved in order to impress a former colleague and new mum crush. Meanwhile, Kevin has a battle with the cloakroom and despite Anne's protests, and Liz takes care of the bar. Comedy about the trials and traumas of middle-class motherhood, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: A pool party ruins Julia's plans before an important event at work. Liz is after a date, and a new Dad on the scene who goes way back with Amanda, puts Kevin's nose out of joint. Comedy, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: While Paul's away he has the perfect solution to help out Julia - he sends his parents to help. Luckily Kevin has a huge fondness for the elderly and assists Julia in her in-law wrangling. Meanwhile, Amanda is having a clearout and reluctantly gives Liz her old coat, which seems to have an effect on Liz. Comedy, starring Anna Maxwell Martin

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Anne's new car propels her into the limelight as the mums try to get in on her new car pool, while Kevin finds himself in the new role of confidant as Amanda shares a deep secret. Liz and Julia's friendship is under fire as Liz realises she is just a 'mum' friend

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Julia's childcare problems are solved after she finds the perfect nanny, but her friendship with Liz is strained. Meanwhile, Amanda is keeping a low profile after Kevin blurted out her secret, and Liz visits Lee's new partner to give her some advice. Elsewhere, the school caretaker is in hospital, but the mums have lost their ring leader and there's nobody to organise a card. Comedy about the trials and traumas of middle-class motherhood, starring Anna Maxwell Martin, Diane Morgan and Lucy Punch

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob speaks to David Hockney about his new work at Westminster Abbey - a towering stained-glass window commissioned to celebrate 65 years since Queen Elizabeth II's coronation there. The artist famously refused a knighthood and declined an invitation to paint the Queen's portrait, so the announcement came as something of a surprising move from an artist whose career has continually defied expectation

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that includes luxurious green foliage and an old, weathered covered bridge placed lovingly among all the beauty

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Professional gambler Norman goes on the warpath and Dale heads for sun-kissed Jamaica for a spot of jet-skiing. Mary has a glimpse into the future and Jane gives another rousing performance _ but will she receive a standing ovation?

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Jim decides to recruit more women into high-ranking government positions - but Sir Humphrey tries to put a stop to his plans. Satirical comedy, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom Good decides he has had enough of commuting - but his solution to the problem causes embarrassment among the well-to-do neighbours. First episode of the classic comedy, starring Richard Briers, Felicity Kendal, Paul Eddington and Penelope Keith

programme.title: Hemingway
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of Ernest Hemingway, considered one of the greatest American writers. The documentary examines Hemingway's enduring influence on literature and culture and also penetrates the myths surrounding him to reveal a deeply troubled, controversial and ultimately tragic figure. The story begins with his childhood in Illinois, his time with the Red Cross during the First World War, his marriage and his move to Paris, where he started work as a writer

programme.title: Michael Palin's Hemingway Adventure
programme.desc: Travel series with the former Monty Python star, this time following in the footsteps of one of his favourite writers, Ernest Hemingway, retracing his journeys across Europe, Africa and America. Michael begins his odyssey in Spain, where he explores bullfighting in Valencia, before heading for Africa to see the spectacular Mount Kilimanjaro

programme.title: Philly DA: Breaking the Law
programme.desc: Krasner's pledge to end the execution of prisoners is put to the test after a police officer is murdered while trying to protect citizens during a robbery

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the role of artists and architects in the development of American cities and culture, as the country sought to step out of Europe's shadow. He travels to Manhattan, Chicago and Massachusetts to examine the work of Norman Rockwell, John Sloan, George Bellows and Louis Sullivan, before charting the links between the Great Depression of the 1920s and 30s and the rise of abstract expressionism. He also visits Jackson Pollock's studio in Long Island and Mark Rothko's Chapel in Houston, Texas

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: It's time for the Year 6 school trip, and Julia, Liz and Meg find themselves on the coach journey from Hell with humourless school secretary Mrs Lamb and clueless teaching assistant Ms Vaughn. It all kicks off when a boy banned for bad behaviour is allowed on the bus, along with his mother Ashley, and Liz decides to play the hero. Meanwhile, Kevin takes his driving test and Amanda puts her lifestyle brand Hygge Tygge online

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: As the school year begins, a new mum at the gates attracts the attention of Julia, Liz and Kevin, with Amanda telling them she is a high-flying businesswoman with five kids and a sexy husband. Julia is struggling to keep her head above water at work when she is offered the choice of promotion or redundancy. Comedy about the trials and traumas of motherhood, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Having resigned from her job in PR, Julia tries to adapt to life as a freelancer and reluctantly agrees to help Amanda launch her new shop - even though she believes it is nothing but a vanity project. The opening is a success, but a careless online post threatens to destroy her good work. Meanwhile, Liz rails against the gentrification of the area and the loss of her beloved kebab shop, while Kevin reveals he has written an unusual children's book, which he wants Amanda to sell in her shop

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Julia finds that working from home is impossible due to all the tasks she has to do for her family. Meg tells her this is her 'Mother's Load' - all the things that no-one else will do, but need to be done to keep the home running. Julia removes herself to the cafe to work, but even there finds it's not that easy to get motivated. A possible solution presents itself when she shares a table with a fellow freelancer, but is Julia taking advantage of him? Meanwhile, much to her surprise, Liz finds herself bored to tears when her youngest starts nursery and she is alone during the day, and Kevin has problems when he takes on responsibility for a school fund-raising project

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Kevin reluctantly hosts a last-minute Halloween party, while Julia takes the kids trick-or-treating in an attempt to meet a celebrity who has just moved into the neighbourhood. Liz tags along as she has been stood up by a date and Anne is in attendance trying to make sure that the kids are safe - but by the time they reach Kevin's house one of the children is missing

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Everyone is delighted when Kevin books a cottage for them to spend half term in the countryside with all their children. But the house is not as nice as the pictures online suggested, and it is not long before tempers begin to fray. Anna Maxwell Martin, Diane Morgan and Paul Ready star

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a peaceful landscape, with distant foothills and velvety shrubs surrounding the quiet waters of an inlet

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Chronicle of life on board the luxurious Galaxy cruise ship. Singer Jane visits a medium, casino workers Dale and Mary celebrate their wedding anniversary by having a go at rollerblading and Jack steps into the shoes of injured lead dancer Philip - will he learn the routine in time?

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Using clips from the science strand's archive, this film examines how people have learnt to live with new emerging technology of the time, and how artificial intelligence systems power our daily lives

programme.title: The Joy of AI
programme.desc: Professor Jim Al-Khalili looks at how man has created machines that can simulate, augment, and even outperform the human mind - and why this shouldn't cause alarm. He reveals the story of the pursuit of AI, the emergence of machine learning and the recent breakthroughs brought about by artificial neural networks. He shows how AI is not only changing the world, but also challenging man's very ideas of intelligence and consciousness. Along the way, the programme investigates spam filters, meets a cutting-edge chatbot, looks at why a few altered pixels makes a computer think it's looking at a trombone rather than a dog

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: A sensitive and moving account of a year in the life of Sasha, a seven-year-old French girl assigned male at birth, and her family's fight for her acceptance. The film follows her parents as they come to terms with their daughter's gender dysmorphia and reveals how Sasha is affected by the societal norms that make it far from easy for her to experience childhood in the same way as most of her peers

programme.title: Prejudice and Pride: The People's History of LGBTQ Britain
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary in which people share the items that have defined their lives in the 50 years since homosexuality was partially decriminalised in 1967. They include a rare collection of the first openly gay magazine - featuring the rising young singer David Bowie - to letters from worried parents of newly 'out' daughters and sons. Presented by Stephen K Amos and Susan Calman

programme.title: Prejudice and Pride: The People's History of LGBTQ Britain
programme.desc: The second of two documentaries in which people share the items that have defined their lives in the 50 years since homosexuality was partially decriminalised in 1967. Focusing on the past 30 years, it features the nun-impersonating freedom-fighting Sisters of Perpetual Indulgence, the writer behind a famous lesbian kiss and a Muslim man who set up an LGBTQ support group for South East Asians. Presented by Susan Calman and Stephen K Amos

programme.title: The Joy of Painting

programme.title: The Cruise

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Julia struggles to abide by the rules and clashes with Mrs Lamb as she attends her first-ever school sports day, where apparently it's no longer acceptable to cheer for individual children and spectators can only offer generic support like 'good job'. Over at the Kidiverse soft-play centre, Kevin is devastated that an important work presentation means he will miss the dads' race. Liz worries that her new boyfriend is blanking her, and Anne discovers something that leads to a nasty surprise for Amanda

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed takes viewers on a remarkable journey to places rarely seen as she travels through Iran to tell the story of its complex and fascinating people, culture and history. She begins by visiting places rarely seen by Western audiences to reveal how the Persian empire was born, the mysterious writing of its early people and how its powerful kings built a vast realm that became the envy of the ancient world

Back at HQ Penfold is tasked with protecting the entire world from the threat of super-villainy. Stripped off his gadgets, DM struggles to adapt to life in the wilderness and, when a talking pinecone called Conni begins to offer him survival tips, he begins to question his own sanity. programme.desc: Community on Solid Ground is a youth centre in Whalley Range. Before its current management took over the building the area didn't offer much for the youngsters living there and anti-social behaviour was very common. That has dropped by 73 per cent since Community on Solid Ground came along, the kids can hang out, play sports and have a place to go that means they aren't hanging around the streets. Sadly the facilities are very tired and they don't have areas they can play their beloved sport on, so the Dengineers are going to transform their outside space into a sports arena worthy of the Olympics. programme.title: Would You Rather
programme.desc: Would You Rather? is a brand new series for CBBC hosted by comedian Darren Harriott. In this episode, Darren is joined by social media sensations Adam Beales and Joe Tasker, alongside his usual crew of 15 kids, as they face some big and bamboozling dilemmas to debate. Our celebrity mates Adam and Joe are tasked with trying to think just like Darren’s Crew and guess which way they vote on a series of hilarious Would You Rather? dilemmas. This episode features fun dilemmas, such as Would You Rather… Have ears the size of an elephant or a trunk instead of a nose? Have hair that changes colour depending on the temperature or have eyes that change colour depending on your mood? Time Travel into the past or time travel into the future? programme.desc: Lars and Kyan swim through the shallows of Crystal River in Florida. Kyan tests his new swimming fin, while Lars watches a manatee and her baby cruise by. Xuli is water-skiing further downstream in the open waters of Crystal Bay, while Foz and Ubercorn watch on from Jet Pad. Grandmaster Glitch is taking the tourist family on a mermaid spotting tour of Crystal River aboard his Grimmer-boat. As Glitch heads off in search of real mermaids, he accidentally startles the mother manatee. Ubercorn realises the manatee will be in danger from the speeding boats and from the cold deep water. The Go Jetters need to mount a manatee rescue quickly. While Xuli attempts to stop the speedboats, Foz launches the Vroomster. The baby manatee has become attached to Kyan, so Lars suggests he remains with her, while Lars and Foz go to save the mother. Kyan is not happy at having to play baby-sitter.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are going to Westman Islands, Iceland, where the world’s largest colony of Atlantic puffins gathers every year. Lars loves these tough little birds and explains how, although they spend most of their lives flying out to sea and fishing, they return to land each year to lay their eggs. When the baby birds, also called pufflings, eventually hatch, they fly out towards the ocean. But the light from nearby towns often confuses them about where to go, leading local children and adults to form 'puffin patrols' to rescue the pufflings. Lars is excited to help out but is upset when he discovers Glitch, also a fan of puffins, is already well-known on the islands for his skills as a puffin rescuer. Although Glitch offers him a place in his puffin patrol, Lars is determined to outdo Glitch with a patrol of his own - but when he recruits a clumsy Grimbot, things take a turn for the worse...

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie is having a party! She finds out how bubbles work when we blow into bubble mixture. She shows us how the surface tension of water changes when we add soap and how we can stop bubbles from bursting with our hands, and using her slow-motion camera, she shows how bubbles make a sphere shape. Next, Maddie heads to a factory where a party favourite is made - chocolate! We learn how chocolate starts out as a fruit, and we see the chocolates being made for an assortment box. Maddie also uses her special camera to see the super-fast robots that put the different chocolates into the right holes in their boxes.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are in Australia, exploring the Wolfe Creek Crater, also known as Kandimalal. While Ubercorn explains how the crater was formed by a meteorite, Foz searches it for fragments of the space rock, as objects from space often contain precous metals. But soon he has competition in the form of Glitch, who races Foz to find an object that both of their metal detectors pick up - which turns out to be a rusty spoon! In the end, neither of them manage to find any pieces of meteorite, prompting Glitch to send the Grimbots to drag a huge space rock to Earth to help him! Can the Go Jetters prevent this huge disaster before it's too late?

programme.desc: The Squirrels ask Duggee if they can grow something when he sows seeds, they decide on onions, carrots, cucumber, pumpkins and strawberries

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters face their toughest mission yet: organising a birthday party for Ubercorn without him finding out! Luckily, when Glitch and Ubercorn were cadets together, they made a map of all the secret passages around the Academy. The Go Jetters can use it to avoid detection while they prepare the party. Xuli and Lars take a secret tunnel to the arts and craft room to make the decorations, while Foz, Kyan and Glitch head out into the Academy garden to gather Ubercorn’s favourite flowers, cherry blossoms. But things don't go according to plan, and the Go Jetters have to hide themselves to avoid a wandering Ubercorn.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter accidentally discards his mother's most precious possessions, and sets out to rescue them from the clutches of Tommy Brock

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Shellington and the Octonauts attempt to track down an unusual jellyfish that can seemingly live for ever

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Three's attempts to hide in the Clubhouse get increasingly tricky in a slapstick comedy of Threes! Learn which numbers are multiples of three, with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster heads out to meet up with Book Cub, who has a special Super Sound Day surprise for him. Before he meets her, he hears a buzzing bee and has fun beatboxing to it.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Pirate R takes her friends on a pretend sea-faring adventure. Fun and games with the 26 letters which come to life, tell stories and make words

programme.title: Kit and Pup
programme.desc: Let's find out about the world with Kit and Pup! Kit and Pup are roller-skating in the park. Can you see who is skating on the rough path and who is skating on the smooth path?

programme.title: Maddie, the Home and You
programme.desc: Children's programme.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Baby monkey Tee gets involved in cake-making, but gets more on his face than he does in the baking tin, so Mo tries to clean him up with a face wipe

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The friends dress up, trying on party clothing and silly costumes

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘grass’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. Today, we see lots of grass – green grass, soft grass, spikey grass, growing grass and mowing grass. We see a pair of hairy grass heads, a group of groovy grasses and Dee finds some fairy grass! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Although they have a wonderful submarine, the Kraken is very clever and can camouflage itself. Our friends use a sandwich to draw the animal out and get some great photos.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The little rabbit is upset when his toy train breaks while playing at the playground

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos watch someone try to build a picnic bench, and when she takes a break to walk her dog, they decide to take over

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The quartet get very messy when the Tubby Custard Machine takes them on a Tubby Custard Ride which splats Tubby Custard everywhere. Meanwhile, the children in Tummy Tales have messy fun with art in the park

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee has a big paddling pool and enlists the help of Ethel the elephant in blowing it up and filling it with water so the gang can have fun

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: Meepa takes a tumble and hurts her hand. After a visit to Nurse Sneezle she realises it's good to help monsters who feel poorly and becomes Class Nurse for the day

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: To complete this mission B.O.T. must chase after a choir of Singing Fluffs as they keep floating away from the tuneless Flying Squawker, who is keen to join in with their singing!

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Finding breakfast takes practice for young animals, and Andy presents a young sea otter watching its mother crack into shellfish and a monkey learn how to crack into tasty nuts

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to treasure island to play at being pirates.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore dancing astronauts and a balloon painting, and watch as Book Boy makes a bicycle.

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: It is Sid's turn for a legal adventure when he steps through the Magic Curtains to be a judge, and finds himself presiding over the case of a stolen ring

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Bobble loves playing the copying game, but does not like being copied himself

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world. The Go Jetters try to fix the Eiffel tower after Grandmaster Glitch breaks it while using it as a TV aerial

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels 70 million years back in time on a mission to replace a broken museum artifact, and meets the armoured tank of the dinosaur world, Ankylosaur

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: t's Waffle's birthday and Mr Willow throws a huge party for him. As the Brooklyn-Bells prepare a surprise for Waffle, Connie and Ruby's dog, Jelly, leads the Wonder Dog astray

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Jack recalls the time he helped a sea turtle hatch her eggs

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Tig visits a restaurant, but is put off eating a new dish when she tastes an olive

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘grass’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. Today, we see lots of grass – green grass, soft grass, spikey grass, growing grass and mowing grass. We see a pair of hairy grass heads, a group of groovy grasses and Dee finds some fairy grass! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: H and friends look after a runaway egg and find it sitting on a wall

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Three's attempts to hide in the Clubhouse get increasingly tricky in a slapstick comedy of Threes! Learn which numbers are multiples of three, with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: But while he is showing them what he can do, he accidentally rubs them out! Pug helps the pencil get them back to their original design.

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy and friends have to get Lofty's heart pumping so they can celebrate his birthday. Science-educational series following a curious, adventure-seeking and unruly monster as he finds the answers to everything a pre-school child would want to know

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: We learn about objects that are associated with the colour black, from blackbirds to blackberries, as well as explore how black patterns are used. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour black.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends visit a magic castle, and there's magic with The Great Tumbleeny!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Laa-Laa and Po laugh themselves silly with a funny joke but when they tell it to Dipsy it doesn't tickle Dipsy in the same way. But Dipsy's funny dance does and when Dipsy dances again the Teletubbies all laugh together. In Tummy Tales, children have fun being silly in a park

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny hides Sula's sparkly shoe in the toilet when the pair want to carry on playing together, but his friend may have to hop home when the footwear gets wet

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch creates trouble with a little pyramid when he and the Go Jetters visit the Pyramids of Giza in Egypt

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb's friends make a cake for his birthday, but he gets carried away when sneakily tasting it, and leaves only a pile of crumbs

programme.title: Footy Pups
programme.desc: Join Rachel and her team as they learn all about 'working as a team'. At Pupton Park, the Footy Pups squabble over the captain's armband

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook and Line build a den for Captain Captain

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog's newspaper boat blows away and the squirrels race to rescue it

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The friends volunteer to deliver Mrs Tiggy-Winkle's laundry using their new superfast go-kart, meaning a routine delivery turns into a high-speed adventure

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster arrives at the Lost Things Office ready to help Elder Bunny try and reunite the citizens of Fluffytown with possessions they might have lost.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The gang uses a new wave music video to explore science

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: CBeebies presenter Andy is jetting off all over the world and meeting amazing animals living in and nearby water. Get ready to explore oceans, rivers and lakes

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie is finding out about things that go up and down. She shows us how a lift works, when it carries people up and down between floors of a building. With the help of an engineer and her special cameras, Maddie show us how the gears and weights are controlled by a computer. Next, it's time for lift-off as Maddie heads to a space museum. We see how a spacesuit is made from lots of different layers of special materials to keep astronauts safe when they are working in space, and Maddie uses her special thermal camera to see how the suit keeps astronauts warm.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly examines her shelf of special things at the back of Mack’s Stall. She chooses the photo of Bob and Ted and remembers the day she got it. When Bob’s identical twin, Ted, arrives at the Big Hub for a visit, Molly and Mack can’t believe how alike they are. Bob and Ted tell them how they used to pretend to be one another when they were boys. This gives Molly an idea! Bob and Ted could play the same trick again, on everyone in the Hub. Soon, Molly and Bob are up on the balcony watching Ted pretending to be Bob at his stall. At first, the plan goes well, but things soon start to go wrong. Ted gets everyone’s names mixed up and he doesn’t know his plums from his apricots. Finally, when Moira asks ‘Bob’ to fix her spinny chair, we discover that Ted is a bit useless at mending things, and the game is most definitely up!

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie, Waffle, Ruby and Jelly have a playdate, and Mrs Hobbs has a delivery shop disaster

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When Gran Gran and JoJo find a family recipe for Great Gran Gran's signature Saint Lucian banana bread, they're excited to bake a batch of it! But there's one problem - cake mix has been spilt on the recipe, so they don't know how long to bake it for!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Duck has been leaving his toys everywhere, so Sarah decides to help him tidy his bedroom

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Colly Wobble has lost her shoes. But in Storyland, Moon Baby shows her a magic shoe tree!

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major gives three froglets medals for helping him, but the other froglets are jealous

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Ninky Nonk leaves without the Tombliboos, forcing them to chase after it. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Frank Skinner reads There's a Lion in My Cornflakes, by Michelle Robinson and Jim Field
