programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the opening day from the All England Club, as the first round continues in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. There was a major upset on Centre Court on day one when the tournament was last held in 2019, with Yulia Putintseva defeating second seed Naomi Osaka 7-6, 6-2. This was followed by a straight-sets victory for home favourite Kyle Edmund over Jaume Munar, and the London resident will hope the support of the fans can help him to go far this time around. Elsewhere, Karolina Pliskova followed up her triumph at Eastbourne with a 6-2, 7-6 victory over Zhu Lin, and Coco Gauff demonstrated her enormous potential when she prevailed 6-4, 6-4 against five-time champion Venus Williams at the age of just 15

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bernie tells Tiffany the weight-loss pills are not working fast enough and she needs stronger ones but her friend refuses. Vi encourages Stuart and Rainie to find a distraction from Abi, prompting them to make a decision about the surrogacy. Rocky has an idea to bring Mila and Iqra back together, while Isaac tells Lola he has a surprise for her

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany reluctantly gives more pills to Bernie, who is disappointed to discover she has not lost as much weight as she had hoped - convincing her she has blown her chance of becoming Rainie's surrogate. Isaac pays a visit to Ruby at the club - and he has plenty to say - and Kathy helps to clear the air between Mila and Iqra

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Lucky tries to overcome her personal crisis by throwing herself into work. But in her eagerness to go above and beyond she oversteps the mark. A patient accuses Dominic of leaving an unwanted item in his body during an operation, and Kylie put her foot in it with her flatmates

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design an Ibiza-themed party garden for a family in Wokingham, Berkshire. All concerned hope to come up with some cool ideas to transform this unloved plot into a laid-back Balearic chill out lounge on a budget of £3,000

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Olivia Colman finds some surprises in her ancestral history, and her belief that most of those in her family tree emanate from Norfolk turns out to be far from the truth. Olivia is astonished to discover that she needs to travel to India to find out more about her great-great-great grandmother Harriot, who was an orphan in 1811 on a ship bound for England from Calcutta. Harriot's father was an an Englishman, but the identity of her mother, who gave birth to her in a remote Indian village, is a mystery Olivia delights in solving

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Grime artist Saskilla concludes the series with a look at ongoing issues with diversity in the music industry, questioning the long-term benefits of the Blackout Tuesday social media campaign and if the money pledged by major labels is enough to bring about lasting change

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the fifth and sixth last-16 matches, which were Croatia v Spain at Parken in Copenhagen and France v Switzerland at National Arena Bucharest

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Citizen Khan star Adil Ray hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Conservative MP Nicky Morgan joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: France v Switzerland. A chance to see the sixth match in the last 16, which took place at National Arena Bucharest

programme.desc: At the UK's most remote hospital, the Gilbert Bain, expectant mum Kirsti prepares to give birth by C-section with the help of the theatre team

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the second day from the All England Club, as the first round continues in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. Roger Federer began his bid for a record-extending ninth Wimbledon title at this stage in 2019 and surprisingly lost the first set to Lloyd Harris, before turning on the style to prevail 3-6, 6-1, 6-2, 6-2. Another Wimbledon great was next up on Centre Court, with Serena Williams winning 6-2, 7-5 against qualifier Giulia Gatto-Monticone, before fourth seed Kiki Bertens secured a comfortable 6-3, 6-2 victory over Mandy Minella

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: England v Germany (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the seventh and penultimate last-16 match, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. This mouthwatering clash will bring back all sorts of memories for both nations of their previous high-profile meetings, and England manager Gareth Southgate will also be keen to lead his side to victory, having famously missed in the penalty shoot-out against Germany in the Euro 96 semi-final. England's 1-0 win over Czech Republic in their final group match helped them to first place in Group D without conceding a goal, despite not playing at their best, but they will still be confident against a German side that shipped five goals in their fixtures against France, Portugal and Hungary

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Sweden v Ukraine (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the eighth and final last-16 fixture, as the quarter-final line-up is decided at Hampden Park in Glasgow. The Swedes topped Group E after drawing with pre-tournament pool favourites Spain in their opener, before overcoming Slovakia 1-0 and then edging a pulsating encounter with Poland 3-2 thanks to Emil Forsberg's second and third goals of the tournament and an injury-time winner courtesy of Viktor Claesson. Ukraine seemed set for an early exit, having lost to the Netherlands and Austria, but other results went their way and they were the only third-placed side to progress with just three points

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne offer their own unique take on the tournament, reacting to the final last-16 matches and looking forward to the quarter-finals, with celebrity guests and a few surprises

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Dylan's search for Emma's mother turns up troublesome results, and he is forced to confront the difficult truth of his findings. An unexpected discovery brings Norman home. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma finds herself torn between Romero and her son, while Norman realises just how much everyone has changed in his absence. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see one of the final two last-16 matches - either England v Germany at Wembley Stadium or Sweden v Ukraine at Hampden Park

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the stories of a quirky property in Fulham, a huge renovation in Tamworth, Staffordshire, and a terrace in Bradford

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the third day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, featuring the opening second-round action from the outside courts

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents further coverage of the third day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, featuring more second-round matches. There was heartbreak for British number one Kyle Edmund at this stage two years ago when he was beaten in five sets by experienced Spaniard Fernando Verdasco. That match was followed on Centre Court by a comfortable 6-3, 6-2, 6-2 victory for eventual champion Novak Djokovic over Denis Kudla

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen references clips from half a century and more of Welsh television as he sets out on a quest to discover the truth about rugby. It's a story of vodka, Groggs and adventures away, full of lively characters from the nation's sporting past

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage musical instrument expert Pete Woods takes on a tarnished, dented saxophone that once played to lift people's spirits during the Blitz, but has not made a sound in more than 40 years. Silversmith Brenton West's skills are tested when he is handed an 80-year-old brass kettle in desperate need of professional attention. Saddler Suzie Fletcher restores a leather shaving mirror used by troops in the trenches of the First World War, and metal-worker Dominic Chinea sharpens up his act with a vintage carpenter's fret saw that has seen better days

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Bad Influencer: The Great Insta Con
programme.desc: The rise and fall of internet wellness guru Belle Gibson, who cultivated an adoring online following with the story that she had successfully treated her own cancer by eating a plant-based diet and using alternative natural therapies. There was one major problem with Belle's story - she never had cancer

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. First shown as Henry's home country prepared to host Euro 2016 following November 2015's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the second day from the All England Club, as the first round continues in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. Roger Federer began his bid for a record-extending ninth Wimbledon title at this stage in 2019 and surprisingly lost the first set to Lloyd Harris, before turning on the style to prevail 3-6, 6-1, 6-2, 6-2. Another Wimbledon great was next up on Centre Court, with Serena Williams winning 6-2, 7-5 against qualifier Giulia Gatto-Monticone, before fourth seed Kiki Bertens secured a comfortable 6-3, 6-2 victory over Mandy Minella

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: England v Germany (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the seventh and penultimate last-16 match, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. This mouthwatering clash will bring back all sorts of memories for both nations of their previous high-profile meetings, and England manager Gareth Southgate will also be keen to lead his side to victory, having famously missed in the penalty shoot-out against Germany in the Euro 96 semi-final. England's 1-0 win over Czech Republic in their final group match helped them to first place in Group D without conceding a goal, despite not playing at their best, but they will still be confident against a German side that shipped five goals in their fixtures against France, Portugal and Hungary

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Sweden v Ukraine (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the eighth and final last-16 fixture, as the quarter-final line-up is decided at Hampden Park in Glasgow. The Swedes topped Group E after drawing with pre-tournament pool favourites Spain in their opener, before overcoming Slovakia 1-0 and then edging a pulsating encounter with Poland 3-2 thanks to Emil Forsberg's second and third goals of the tournament and an injury-time winner courtesy of Viktor Claesson. Ukraine seemed set for an early exit, having lost to the Netherlands and Austria, but other results went their way and they were the only third-placed side to progress with just three points

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne offer their own unique take on the tournament, reacting to the final last-16 matches and looking forward to the quarter-finals, with celebrity guests and a few surprises

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Dylan's search for Emma's mother turns up troublesome results, and he is forced to confront the difficult truth of his findings. An unexpected discovery brings Norman home. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma finds herself torn between Romero and her son, while Norman realises just how much everyone has changed in his absence. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see one of the final two last-16 matches - either England v Germany at Wembley Stadium or Sweden v Ukraine at Hampden Park

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the stories of a quirky property in Fulham, a huge renovation in Tamworth, Staffordshire, and a terrace in Bradford

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the third day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, featuring the opening second-round action from the outside courts

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents further coverage of the third day of the Grand Slam from the All England Club, featuring more second-round matches. There was heartbreak for British number one Kyle Edmund at this stage two years ago when he was beaten in five sets by experienced Spaniard Fernando Verdasco. That match was followed on Centre Court by a comfortable 6-3, 6-2, 6-2 victory for eventual champion Novak Djokovic over Denis Kudla

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Eve faces an explosive showdown with Cameron as his reign of terror reaches a terrifying climax. Amber panics when Rory drops a bombshell, and Jac becomes a target of hate, while Scarlett fusses over Stevie

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage musical instrument expert Pete Woods takes on a tarnished, dented saxophone that once played to lift people's spirits during the Blitz, but has not made a sound in more than 40 years. Silversmith Brenton West's skills are tested when he is handed an 80-year-old brass kettle in desperate need of professional attention. Saddler Suzie Fletcher restores a leather shaving mirror used by troops in the trenches of the First World War, and metal-worker Dominic Chinea sharpens up his act with a vintage carpenter's fret saw that has seen better days

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Heritage Society is re-enacting the French invasion of the island when its president dies. At first it looks like an extreme reaction to a dodgy stew as the rest of the group have fallen ill - but when lab reports reveal there was poison in the pot, Humphrey is left wondering why the victim was the only one to end up in the mortuary. Meanwhile, Dwayne takes Humphrey out on the town to show him his own way of celebrating the founding of Saint Marie, and the DI continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring David Bamber and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Bad Influencer: The Great Insta Con
programme.desc: The rise and fall of internet wellness guru Belle Gibson, who cultivated an adoring online following with the story that she had successfully treated her own cancer by eating a plant-based diet and using alternative natural therapies. There was one major problem with Belle's story - she never had cancer

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. First shown as Henry's home country prepared to host Euro 2016 following November 2015's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: The best of the week's entertainment news with Amy Irons and David Farrell

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a former bank and a two-bed semi in Lancashire, and a one bedroom flat with a garage in Levenshulme, Manchester

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine the case of an 18-year-old hotel employee who was accused of murdering a titled guest in 1922

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts the show from Norfolk's biggest Antique Centre in King's Lynn. Experts Caroline Hawley and Mark Stacey assist the Reds and the Blues as they do battle

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the opening day from the All England Club, as the first round continues in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. There was a major upset on Centre Court on day one when the tournament was last held in 2019, with Yulia Putintseva defeating second seed Naomi Osaka 7-6, 6-2. This was followed by a straight-sets victory for home favourite Kyle Edmund over Jaume Munar, and the London resident will hope the support of the fans can help him to go far this time around. Elsewhere, Karolina Pliskova followed up her triumph at Eastbourne with a 6-2, 7-6 victory over Zhu Lin, and Coco Gauff demonstrated her enormous potential when she prevailed 6-4, 6-4 against five-time champion Venus Williams at the age of just 15

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bernie tells Tiffany the weight-loss pills are not working fast enough and she needs stronger ones but her friend refuses. Vi encourages Stuart and Rainie to find a distraction from Abi, prompting them to make a decision about the surrogacy. Rocky has an idea to bring Mila and Iqra back together, while Isaac tells Lola he has a surprise for her

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany reluctantly gives more pills to Bernie, who is disappointed to discover she has not lost as much weight as she had hoped - convincing her she has blown her chance of becoming Rainie's surrogate. Isaac pays a visit to Ruby at the club - and he has plenty to say - and Kathy helps to clear the air between Mila and Iqra

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Lucky tries to overcome her personal crisis by throwing herself into work. But in her eagerness to go above and beyond she oversteps the mark. A patient accuses Dominic of leaving an unwanted item in his body during an operation, and Kylie put her foot in it with her flatmates

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design an Ibiza-themed party garden for a family in Wokingham, Berkshire. All concerned hope to come up with some cool ideas to transform this unloved plot into a laid-back Balearic chill out lounge on a budget of £3,000

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Olivia Colman finds some surprises in her ancestral history, and her belief that most of those in her family tree emanate from Norfolk turns out to be far from the truth. Olivia is astonished to discover that she needs to travel to India to find out more about her great-great-great grandmother Harriot, who was an orphan in 1811 on a ship bound for England from Calcutta. Harriot's father was an an Englishman, but the identity of her mother, who gave birth to her in a remote Indian village, is a mystery Olivia delights in solving

programme.title: Countryside College
programme.desc: After the Christmas break the CAFRE students are back for another busy term, which offers new life and new experiences on the campuses. At the Enniskillen campus, well-known jockey and three-time Cheltenham winner Derek O'Connor is visiting the equine students to share his top tips

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Grime artist Saskilla concludes the series with a look at ongoing issues with diversity in the music industry, questioning the long-term benefits of the Blackout Tuesday social media campaign and if the money pledged by major labels is enough to bring about lasting change

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the fifth and sixth last-16 matches, which were Croatia v Spain at Parken in Copenhagen and France v Switzerland at National Arena Bucharest

programme.title: Barra on the Foyle
programme.desc: Barra Best makes a journey across the river network, meeting people who work, live and play along its banks,and exploring places and landscapes rarely seen on TV screens

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: France v Switzerland. A chance to see the sixth match in the last 16, which took place at National Arena Bucharest

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate a lethal shooting from 1937 in which a barge captain was allegedly murdered by his best friend

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: At the UK's most remote hospital, the Gilbert Bain, expectant mum Kirsti prepares to give birth by C-section with the help of the theatre team

programme.title: Ulster to the Arctic: A Gentleman's Adventure
programme.desc: In June 1856, The 1st Marquess of Dufferin and Ava, Frederick Hamilton-Temple-Blackwood, left his home in Bangor to travel across Scotland and on to the Arctic in a small sailing schooner. After travelling 6,000 miles in four months, he published an account of his journey and became the most outstanding diplomat of the Victorian era. Now adventurer and travel writer Leon McCarron recreates this seminal voyage by following the accounts written in Lord Dufferin's book, introducing viewers to some of his hero's stories

programme.title: Bad Influencer: The Great Insta Con

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Croatia v Spain (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the fifth last-16 match, which takes place at Parken in Copenhagen. Croatia's victory over Scotland in their final group game helped them to finish second in Group D despite them losing 1-0 to England and drawing with the Czech Republic. Three-time winners Spain were hit by Covid in their camp and drew their opening two group fixtures against Sweden and Poland, but came into form in their match against Slovakia, recording an impressive 5-0 victory to finish second in Group E behind the Swedes

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: France v Switzerland (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the sixth match in the last 16, which takes place at National Arena Bucharest. Group F was the dreaded 'Group of Death' on paper, with holders Portugal, world champions France and three-time winners Germany drawn together alongside Hungary, and it was the French who finished top of the pile despite their only victory coming in their opener against Germany. Switzerland also only won one of their group matches, against Turkey, and will start as heavy underdogs against one of the tournament favourites

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia visits the lush Ionian island of Corfu, beginning in its capital, Corfu Town, where she discovers a surprising, cosmopolitan city more like a little slice of Italy than Greece. After sampling the delights of its famous mumquat flavoured ice-cream, a cookery lesson in making signature savoury dish pastitsada reveals the island's centuries-old love affair with exotic spices. Julia meets a pair of brothers passionate about revolutionising production of Corfu's oldest and most important export - olive oil- and ends her stay trying out the secret to staying young that all Corfiots swear by

programme.title: Extraordinary Twins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary following Nick and Chelsea from Idaho, whose twin daughters Callie and Carter are joined together at the sternum. The programme meets the family as the twins approach their fourth birthday, and their parents face the agonising decision of whether to put them through complex surgery to separate them. To help them make this fateful decision, the family heads on an emotional journey to meet other conjoined twins and their parents. Narrated by Sheridan Smith

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Croatia v Spain (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the fifth last-16 match, which takes place at Parken in Copenhagen. Croatia's victory over Scotland in their final group game helped them to finish second in Group D despite them losing 1-0 to England and drawing with the Czech Republic. Three-time winners Spain were hit by Covid in their camp and drew their opening two group fixtures against Sweden and Poland, but came into form in their match against Slovakia, recording an impressive 5-0 victory to finish second in Group E behind the Swedes

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: France v Switzerland (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the sixth match in the last 16, which takes place at National Arena Bucharest. Group F was the dreaded 'Group of Death' on paper, with holders Portugal, world champions France and three-time winners Germany drawn together alongside Hungary, and it was the French who finished top of the pile despite their only victory coming in their opener against Germany. Switzerland also only won one of their group matches, against Turkey, and will start as heavy underdogs against one of the tournament favourites

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia visits the lush Ionian island of Corfu, beginning in its capital, Corfu Town, where she discovers a surprising, cosmopolitan city more like a little slice of Italy than Greece. After sampling the delights of its famous mumquat flavoured ice-cream, a cookery lesson in making signature savoury dish pastitsada reveals the island's centuries-old love affair with exotic spices. Julia meets a pair of brothers passionate about revolutionising production of Corfu's oldest and most important export - olive oil- and ends her stay trying out the secret to staying young that all Corfiots swear by

programme.title: Extraordinary Twins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary following Nick and Chelsea from Idaho, whose twin daughters Callie and Carter are joined together at the sternum. The programme meets the family as the twins approach their fourth birthday, and their parents face the agonising decision of whether to put them through complex surgery to separate them. To help them make this fateful decision, the family heads on an emotional journey to meet other conjoined twins and their parents. Narrated by Sheridan Smith

programme.desc: Rageh Omaar's monthly series continues with Ben Chapman in Spain's popular holiday destination, Gran Canaria, where the pandemic has left many tourism businesses close to collapse. There, he finds many are also worried about the impact of the continuing influx of migrants arriving by boat from Africa - both on the authorities' ability to cope and on the island's image once the holidaymakers return. And he meets a hotelier couple who have recently taken in a large group of asylum seekers

programme.desc: Roy's nightmares cause concern from Kelly. Zoe wants to face the village over the lorry affair. Roy tells Kelly that he is keen on having kids in the future

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick helps a woman claiming she is being harassed by a mysterious stalker, and discovers the culprit is closer to home than anticipated. Greengrass gets up to his old tricks again - this time trying to persuade a group of wealthy Australians he is a long-lost relative. Drama, starring Nick Berry, Bill Maynard and Juliette Gruber.

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid arranges a fishing weekend, blissfully unaware Jean's father is coming to stay. Classic family comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Owing to a little white lie on his income tax returns, Mr Roper must quickly acquire a son. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy farmer Martin Strickland is discovered bound to a tree, doused in truffle oil and mauled to death by a wild boar, the investigation leads DCI Barnaby to tyrannical celebrity chef Ruth Cameron at the upmarket Wyvern House restaurant. However, secrets from the past surface after the murderer strikes again, and the repercussions are felt further than they were originally intended. With Neil Dudgeon, Neil McCaul, Sharon Small, Marc Elliott and Arabella Weir

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jo is concerned that her father is not managing without his wife and emotions boil over after a car accident. Meanwhile, Greengrass's brother arrives in the village

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emily confides in Butch about her father spending Christmas alone. Detective Spalding uncovers new evidence. Chris decides to attend Liam's funeral

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris infuriates Zoe by telling her he paid for Liam's funeral. Diane flirts with Turner. Paddy and Lisa argue over what to do with the cottage

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Les and Janice grow frustrated as the police hunt for Neil and Toyah. Sally worries about what Kevin has found out. Alec plans to run the Rovers with Rita

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally asks Greg to tell Maxine about them. Les is surprised when Toyah hugs him and thanks him for his support. Jim is thrilled when he feels a twinge in his leg

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective investigates a suspected kidnapping and gets his first lead when a ransom note is discovered. The missing woman then turns up dead at the wheel of a crashed car, smelling of alcohol. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Nelson discover that the exciting world of stunt pilots and military heroes hides many dark secrets after the owner of Finchmere Airfield is hit on the head and dropped to his death from a plane. Are anti-aircraft campaigners behind Bernard King's murder, or is the incident linked to a tragic Second World War mission? Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee are joined by guest stars including June Whitfield, Robert Bathurst, Bernard Cribbins and Laila Rouass

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Ethel's flirting with Major Bryant lands her in trouble as preparations get under way for a concert at Downton. Tensions flare between Isobel and Cora, Edith receives worrying news about Matthew and William, while Branson declares his feelings for Sybil. Mrs Bird's soup kitchen receives a helping hand from Daisy and Mrs Patmore. Costume drama, starring Penelope Wilton and Elizabeth McGovern

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The son of a high-profile Jewish businessman is attacked in the grounds of a university, leaving Foyle to consider the possibility that the attack was racially motivated. Meanwhile, in London, tensions mount as charismatic right-wing leader Guy Spencer is released from prison. But is he being set up to take the spotlight off a dangerous espionage operation involving MI6? Elsewhere, Sam becomes acutely aware of the impact of the war on Adam's constituents as she encounters a young boy suffering from whooping cough. Michael Kitchen stars in the 1940s-set crime drama, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Daniel Weyman

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: The royal family settle into the peace and solitude of Osborne House, their estate on the Isle of Wight, but the outside world is soon making its presence felt as Palmerston stirs up trouble in Westminster. Frustrated at the brewing conflict in parliament, Victoria is forced to summon both Palmerston and Russell to her estate to resolve the situation. On a more domestic front, nocturnal liaisons send gossip rippling through the royal court. Historical drama, starring Jenna Coleman and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: Albert travels to Cambridge to accept a position as Chancellor of the university, leaving Victoria alone as London is ravaged by a deadly cholera epidemic. The crisis leads the queen to an encounter with eccentric physician John Snow, who is determined to end the crisis by discovering how the disease is spread - and a vital breakthrough is provided by a young nurse named Florence Nightingale. The Francatellis host an opening dinner for their new hotel, and Penge notices a growing attraction between Sophie and Joseph

programme.title: Bless This House

Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee are joined by guest stars including June Whitfield, Robert Bathurst, Bernard Cribbins and Laila Rouass

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona apologises to Steve for being so selfish. Jim has pains in his leg and hopes that means it is healing. Sally signs the partnership contract without reading it

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective applies his professional know-how to a fatal shooting, and soon begins to suspect that the dead man's brother-in-law may know more about the case than he is letting on - a theory borne out by forensic tests on the murder weapon. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour detective, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The entire household is shaken by bad news from the front, and Daisy's relationship with William moves faster than she would like. Meanwhile, Mary pays a high price for obtaining Carlisle's assistance when Vera returns to Downton threatening to expose old secrets. Costume drama, starring Sophie McShera, Thomas Howes, Michelle Dockery, Iain Glen and Maria Doyle Kennedy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The last-ever episode. When his colleague Hilda Pierce is targeted by an assassin outside MI5, Foyle must re-examine her top-secret role during the war. He suspects the shooting could be connected to the hunt for a traitor within the Special Operations Executive who may have been behind the deaths of nine agents in France. Meanwhile, Sam is faced with a dilemma when Adam and Glenvil crack down on the black market in East Peckham. Guest stars include Conleth Hill (Game of Thrones' Lord Varys)

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: An attempt is made on the queen's life, and she discovers the would-be assassin was motivated by the political unrest dividing Ireland. Determined to resolve the situation, she arranges a royal visit despite Albert's objections. During the trip, she meets Palmerston's wife and learns more about their unconventional marriage

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: Emotions run wild when the Palace throws an opulent Georgian Ball to celebrate the christening of the queen's seventh child. Victoria feels overwhelmed by events, and looks back over some private etchings that remind her of simpler times. However, she is left distraught when the illustrations are sold to the press. Bertie's behaviour gets out of control, Joseph and Sophie's relationship develops further, and plans are made for a new coin - which both of the royal couple have their own ideas about

programme.desc: Sid returns home from work to be greeted with Sally's shocking news. Family comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is pestered by the sister of Aidensfield's landed gentry, while Greengrass has his sights set in the same direction - poaching on the lord's land

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry begins his job helping Chris and it is not long before Chris is driving him round the bend. Tricia and Lyn both have the hots for Paddy's cousin Jason

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice asks Tricia to be her bridesmaid. Claudia rebuffs Chris's advances. Ashley decides to stage a panto. Paddy is confused as to why Jason is flirting with Tricia

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve asks Maxine not to tell Fiona about what happened. Sally is frustrated by the time it is taking for her to get the girls back. Curly receives a visit from the police

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is charged with trying to extort money from Freshco. Steve accuses Maxine of wrecking Fiona's life. Emily and Spider worry about Curly when he disappears

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Two grieving widows lay rival claims to a corpse found washed up on the beach, each insisting it is the body of her missing husband. While it is obvious only one can be telling the truth, the detective finds it tricky to discern which of the women is lying - and why. Drama, guest starring Eleanor Bron, with Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: A Canadian officer is brought to Downton for recuperation and disrupts everything. Ethel hears distressing news, Bates makes another attempt to settle matters with Vera, while Carson receives a tempting offer. Matthew gets used to life in a wheelchair with Mary as his round-the-clock carer. Costume drama, starring Amy Nuttall, Brendan Coyle, Jim Carter, Dan Stevens and Michelle Dockery

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Return of the drama about two chalk-and-cheese police partners solving crimes on the streets of Bath. In the first episode, five friends take a hot-air balloon trip but only four come back down alive, leaving McDonald and Dodds to untangle a complex web of lies spanning 30 years. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star, with guest appearances from Rob Brydon, Rupert Graves, Martin Kemp and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama. Two seemingly unrelated men are found murdered in different parts of the city on the same day. Are the deaths connected, and if so, could there be a link to the disappearance of a student activist? Commissaris Van der Valk must uncover the truth while also dealing with an over-eager rookie cop joining his tight-knit team. Maimie McCoy, Luke Allen-Gale and Elliot Barnes Worrell co-star

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: Albert unveils ambitious plans for an international exhibition - but has to find a way to fund it. Lord Palmerston causes uproar when he sends the navy in to blockade Greece after an attack on a British subject, and Monmouth hires Penge to spy on Sophie and Joseph - which leads to him discovering revealing information about Joseph's time at Chatsworth

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: The world's eyes are on the Great Exhibition - and the Royal couple. Does triumph or failure beckon? Lives will be changed for ever as intrigue unfolds amongst the exhibits. Drama, charting the early years of the reign of Queen Victoria, witnessing her transformation from the flighty Princess Alexandrina into the formidable monarch, mother and queen who would preside over the construction of an empire. Period drama, starring Jenna Coleman, Tom Hughes, John Sessions, Laurence Fox and Nicholas Audsley

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid and Jean have a lively debate about the war. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid resolves to exercise a little discipline when Sally adopts a stray dog. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid suffers temporary amnesia, but Jean is on hand to produce reminders of his antics at the annual Boy Scouts reunion. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice is upset that Toyah has gone looking for Ronnie. Vera fears she will end up homeless and jobless. Alec takes Rita shopping for wedding rings

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice and Les arrive in London and call on Ronnie. Kevin is surprised when Sally splashes out on his birthday. Jack and Vera agree to Alec buying them out of the pub

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Roy's nightmares cause concern from Kelly. Zoe wants to face the village over the lorry affair. Roy tells Kelly that he is keen on having kids in the future

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick helps a woman claiming she is being harassed by a mysterious stalker, and discovers the culprit is closer to home than anticipated. Greengrass gets up to his old tricks again - this time trying to persuade a group of wealthy Australians he is a long-lost relative. Drama, starring Nick Berry, Bill Maynard and Juliette Gruber.

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid arranges a fishing weekend, blissfully unaware Jean's father is coming to stay. Classic family comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Owing to a little white lie on his income tax returns, Mr Roper must quickly acquire a son. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy farmer Martin Strickland is discovered bound to a tree, doused in truffle oil and mauled to death by a wild boar, the investigation leads DCI Barnaby to tyrannical celebrity chef Ruth Cameron at the upmarket Wyvern House restaurant. However, secrets from the past surface after the murderer strikes again, and the repercussions are felt further than they were originally intended. With Neil Dudgeon, Neil McCaul, Sharon Small, Marc Elliott and Arabella Weir

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jo is concerned that her father is not managing without his wife and emotions boil over after a car accident. Meanwhile, Greengrass's brother arrives in the village

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emily confides in Butch about her father spending Christmas alone. Detective Spalding uncovers new evidence. Chris decides to attend Liam's funeral

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally asks Greg to tell Maxine about them. Les is surprised when Toyah hugs him and thanks him for his support. Jim is thrilled when he feels a twinge in his leg

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective investigates a suspected kidnapping and gets his first lead when a ransom note is discovered. The missing woman then turns up dead at the wheel of a crashed car, smelling of alcohol. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Nelson discover that the exciting world of stunt pilots and military heroes hides many dark secrets after the owner of Finchmere Airfield is hit on the head and dropped to his death from a plane. Are anti-aircraft campaigners behind Bernard King's murder, or is the incident linked to a tragic Second World War mission? Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee are joined by guest stars including June Whitfield, Robert Bathurst, Bernard Cribbins and Laila Rouass

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Ethel's flirting with Major Bryant lands her in trouble as preparations get under way for a concert at Downton. Tensions flare between Isobel and Cora, Edith receives worrying news about Matthew and William, while Branson declares his feelings for Sybil. Mrs Bird's soup kitchen receives a helping hand from Daisy and Mrs Patmore. Costume drama, starring Penelope Wilton and Elizabeth McGovern

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The son of a high-profile Jewish businessman is attacked in the grounds of a university, leaving Foyle to consider the possibility that the attack was racially motivated. Meanwhile, in London, tensions mount as charismatic right-wing leader Guy Spencer is released from prison. But is he being set up to take the spotlight off a dangerous espionage operation involving MI6? Elsewhere, Sam becomes acutely aware of the impact of the war on Adam's constituents as she encounters a young boy suffering from whooping cough. Michael Kitchen stars in the 1940s-set crime drama, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Daniel Weyman

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: The royal family settle into the peace and solitude of Osborne House, their estate on the Isle of Wight, but the outside world is soon making its presence felt as Palmerston stirs up trouble in Westminster. Frustrated at the brewing conflict in parliament, Victoria is forced to summon both Palmerston and Russell to her estate to resolve the situation. On a more domestic front, nocturnal liaisons send gossip rippling through the royal court. Historical drama, starring Jenna Coleman and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: Albert travels to Cambridge to accept a position as Chancellor of the university, leaving Victoria alone as London is ravaged by a deadly cholera epidemic. The crisis leads the queen to an encounter with eccentric physician John Snow, who is determined to end the crisis by discovering how the disease is spread - and a vital breakthrough is provided by a young nurse named Florence Nightingale. The Francatellis host an opening dinner for their new hotel, and Penge notices a growing attraction between Sophie and Joseph

Drama, charting the early years of the reign of Queen Victoria, witnessing her transformation from the flighty Princess Alexandrina into the formidable monarch, mother and queen who would preside over the construction of an empire. Period drama, starring Jenna Coleman, Tom Hughes, John Sessions, Laurence Fox and Nicholas Audsley

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid and Jean have a lively debate about the war. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade visits the Alaskan wilderness in search of the Iliamna Lake monster of native legend that is said to drag people down to an icy grave. Subarctic fishing is a first for the extreme angler, who secures his largest catch to date as he pursues the mysterious creature

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur must find a way of paying a £20,000 tax bill immediately, but fortunately Dave has a drastic solution - faking a drowning accident. Chisholm is suspicious as there is no body, while Arthur is spotted hiding out in a hotel. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: A crook flees justice in the guise of a nun, hotly pursued by Simon Templar - who suddenly finds himself having to take a crash-course in the finer points of flying a stolen experimental plane. Drama, guest starring William Gaunt

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Tara and Steed try and put all the pieces together when people start turning up dead in unique locations with a jigsaw puzzle piece placed on their persons. Peter Jeffrey guest stars

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: When a foreign student, an apparent cocaine trafficker, is shot dead, Cowley tasks Bodie and Doyle with targetting teenage traffickers who have diplomatic ties with South America. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins, Gordon Jackson and Alice Krige

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur unwittingly causes a diplomatic incident by trying to strike a deal with the crew of a visiting Russian ship. Guest starring Rula Lenska, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin introduces some of the great racehorses of the modern era, including Nijinsky, Tiger Roll, Best Mate, Desert Orchid and Shergar

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage two of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 183.5km route from Perros-Guirec to Mr-de-Bretagne Guerledan. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Tour de France Live
programme.desc: Coverage of stage three of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 183km route from Lorient to Pontivy. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In the heart of Central America lurks a marine creature believed to reach 8ft in length and weigh up to 300lbs. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade is taken to breaking point as he re-evaluates everything he knows about fishing in an attempt to catch this monster - which is known to leap into boats and cause fatal injuries

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On the Canada-US border lies a vast lake that hides a dark history. For Native Americans it's an ancient and sacred site, but recently something has risen up from the depths to attack swimmers, and stories abound of an invasion of primordial bloodsuckers. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade plunges into the heart of a waterfall in the Pacific Northwest on a mission to catch the oldest river monster of his career

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole goes green-laning in Oxfordshire, visits speed week in Yorkshire and tells the story of the Streetfighter, one of the most distinctive styles of modern motorbike

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage three of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 183km route from Lorient to Pontivy. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Yorkshire Airport
programme.desc: Michael faces a big day, as for the first time in his career, he is responsible for manoeuvring a 35-tonne plane into the correct position for take-off. Security supervisor Conor finds himself in a sticky situation with a padlock, and there is a look behind the scenes at the baggage hall, where 9,500 items of luggage pass through every day. Baggage agent Johnny is responsible for reuniting each lost item with its owner

programme.title: Yorkshire Airport
programme.desc: The staff prepares for the grand re-opening of the airport after millions have been spent on renovations, and Phil nervously awaits a visit from Transport Secretary Chris Grayling. An oversized piece of hand luggage causes trouble at the gate, and problems arise for Special Assistance Agent involving three special charter pilgrimage planes

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, airport staff are forced to cordon off a plane full of passengers when a runway is closed, and Ian turns detective

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, passenger experience manager Demi deals with a medical emergency. Meanwhile, border force officer Rob returns to cross-examine unsuspecting passengers arriving into the UK

programme.title: Moto GP Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the ninth race of the season, the Motul TT Assen in the Netherlands, as the riders tried to secure crucial points at the TT Assen Circuit in Amsterdam

programme.title: The Purge (2013)
programme.desc: The American government responds to rising crime rates by introducing an annual 12-hour period when all laws are suspended, so the people can get violent impulses out of their system. A family takes shelter at home, but when a stranger turns up asking for help, the mayhem outside soon catches up with them. Horror, starring Lena Headey and Ethan Hawke

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Private investigator Caroline Selby seeks Arthur's help in locating eccentric recluse and inventor Albert Goddard, who has come into an inheritance. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood delves into the ITV Sport archives to take a look back at James Hunt, Barry Sheene and a spectacular crash by Kelvin Burt

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage three of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 183km route from Lorient to Pontivy. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Tour de France Live
programme.desc: Coverage of stage four of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 150.5km route from Redon to Fougeres. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Reports of attacks in a remote part of Ukraine take extreme angler Jeremy Wade on a hunt for an elusive lake monster, and to the site of the world's worst nuclear accident - Chernobyl's reactor number four. Can he reveal the culprit and deliver it to the scientists searching for evidence of mutant species?

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Colombia to search for a fish suspected of dragging a honeymooning bride to her death in the Amazon. A key witness turns the investigation on its head after revealing the body was found with a single puncture wound, and Jeremy conducts an experiment to prove his unlikely new suspect is capable of pulling an adult under the water

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part investigation into the Loch Ness Monster, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets out to examine the evidence, review famous sightings and photographs and talk to eyewitnesses, as he researches the real creature that lies behind the myth

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole meets the California-based Brit who is reviving the American chopper. He also discovers the joys of Soviet-era sidecars, and rides vintage flat tankers in Cornwall

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage four of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 150.5km route from Redon to Fougeres. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Gregg Wallace, Denise Lewis, Mark Pougatch and Gina Yashere pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: An Audience with Billy Connolly
programme.desc: The comedian descends on London to entertain a celebrity crowd including Twiggy, Ringo Starr, Michael Brandon, Samantha Fox and Clive James, covering topics including ageing, parties and his idea for a new national anthem

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The world of marine salvage and rescue in Australia, filmed in the Whitsunday Islands off Queensland

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Now Murtaugh has met his match in the shape of former international CIA agent Wesley Cole, the newly melded partners attempt to find a steady rhythm as they work to solve the murder of a finance executive. Cole, who has returned home to assumes duty as an LAPD officer, also has to navigate his role with his ex and his daughter. Meanwhile, Avery grapples with the outcome of his campaign for city council. Damon Wayans, Seann William Scott and Kevin Rahm star in the fast-paced detective drama based on the hit feature films

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A recently released convict causes chaos when he takes a group of hospital patients hostage. Bodie and Doyle try to get to him before he can start shooting. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: When the Ministry is broken into by an apparently invisible man, it seems a secret formula has fallen into the hands of an organisation run by Soviet Intelligence. Light-hearted action, starring Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is shot by an intruder in his flat, and enters a near coma. Cowley fears a terrorist group - two of whose members were blown up during a recent car chase by CI5 - is responsible. While Doyle experiences surreal dreams involving a ghostly graveyard, Cowley and Bodie attempt to pursue the villain behind the attack. Action drama, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry panics after putting himself in an incriminating position by leaving his fingerprints on a stolen lorry full of electrical items he has collected for Arthur. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star

programme.desc: Simon finds himself teetering between two women racing drivers - who are rivals in love as well as on the track. Adventure, guest starring Kate O'Mara

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Three bodies from Peru, Colombia and Brazil have been recovered with mysterious injuries that look like burn marks, but who or what could have inflicted them? In an attempt to uncover the perpetrator, Jeremy Wade travels further into the Amazon jungle than ever before, enlisting the help of a secretive and little-known tribe called the Matis to shed light on this unusual case

programme.title: World Rally Championship Highlights

programme.title: Quantum of Solace (2008)
programme.desc: James Bond sets out on a personal mission of vengeance as he pursues the secret criminal organisation Quantum. Spy thriller sequel, starring Daniel Craig, Olga Kurylenko, Judi Dench, Gemma Arterton and Mathieu Amalric

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: A wheelwright and his team restore a Victorian bicycle, and a couple compete in a regatta to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the Fowey River Class sailing dinghy. An artisan brewer and wine-maker searches for ingredients for his next creation on the grounds of Torbay's most historic building, and a stonemason and drystone waller gets to work on one of Cornwall's most unique landmarks

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A doctor shares his story of leaving his family and newborn child during lockdown to keep them safe. An 11-year-old hit by a car shows signs of possible internal bleeding, and a 60-year-old woman is injured after tripping over her cat. A footballer being treated for injuries to his achilles tendon describes how the sport has been an escape for him during his childhood in south London

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The realities of policing home drinking, and what happens when parties go very badly wrong, seen through the eyes of South Yorkshire Police's frontline officers. PCs Alice Marshall and Matt Spaven investigate a house party that's a concern for neighbours, while PCs Mohammed Alrobaidi and Bernice Gott find themselves at risk at a rowdy house party in a quiet street. In Rotherham, PCs Pete Ellard and Tabitha Rumney have their hands full with a drunk and disorderly couple who are violently resisting arrest

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics in Bromsgrove treat a 52-year-old man suffering sudden, severe headaches, while Manni Suman and Lucy McGraynor look after an unconscious patient. In Worcester, paramedics Ed Hird and Nick Carson have to negotiate a garden fence to reach a 65-year-old biker with a shattered leg

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay tries to improve a family-owned Irish pub in California, stripping out and refurbishing the dining room, creating a simplified menu and giving staff training

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the north-east of England, married couple Kevin and Lalita kick off the competition with a menu inspired by Italian food and local produce. On day two, local girl Michelle and her southern wife, Charlotte, put on an Asian themed evening. Finally, the oldest couple in the group, loved-up Richard and Christine, host an evening of what they call dinner party classics

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Tom Allen and Liam Charles present as the pastry chefs create miniature cone-shaped and carrot-shaped desserts - 24 of each. For the second challenge they must serve up a grand occasion showpiece - a Religieuse a l'Ancienne - to serve 24 people, with two different flavours of eclairs and inspired by the theme of time travel. Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden judge their efforts

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A no-nonsense Nigerian mum who raises her kids in a strict culture of respect swaps lives with an overwhelmed woman whose disconnected husband and spoilt children run the house

programme.title: Best British Short
programme.desc: Dungarees, written and directed by Abel Rubinstein. Transgender Blake and cisgender Cane hang out, play video games, and grapple with their insecurities

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Seyi Rhodes reports on the migrants being beaten back from the European Union by border guards on the notorious Balkan Route. A once-welcoming Europe is now closing its doors, and Serbia has become a bottleneck for thousands of people trying to get through the increasingly hostile route. Rhodes meets beaten and bruised men trying to leave Serbia, and witnesses the families living in government camps too frightened to make the journey. He also hears how the animosity is fuelling a small but growing right-wing political movement

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a father and son building an ambitious clifftop retreat and a young architect embarking on his first commission - a garden pavilion for his grandmother. programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton is in rural Somerset, where Tom and Laura are building a home on Tom's family's farm - a dream he has had since childhood. But despite years of planning, Charlie discovers a slight issue with the couple's plans - they don't have any

programme.desc: Including sport and weather

Presented by Tom Allen and Liam Charles

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The South Yorkshire Police deal with complex cases of parents living in fear of their own children. In Sheffield, a mother asks for her 15-year-old to be taken into custody after he starts causing damage at their home, and for the second time in three days, the same officers are despatched to a report of a son attacking his father. Elsewhere, a distraught mother is under siege from her violent and aggressive 23-year-old son

programme.title: A Very British Sex Shop
programme.desc: Documentary following the Richardsons, who run a chain of sex shops in Brighton and whose day-to-day dealings provide a unique insight into the bedroom habits of Britain. The film charts the family's efforts to keep business booming, from brainstorms on making the most out of the lucrative Valentine's season to a latex fashion show, starring owner Tim's daughter Kizzy

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes two more people looking for love to take their pick from six nude candidates. Retired dentist Leanne is recently divorced and in search of a sexually experimental man, while former US marine Conor, who falls in love all too easily, is looking for his dream Englishwoman

programme.title: The Super Orgasm
programme.desc: For most women multiple orgasms can seem almost mythical. But some can experience it not once, twice or even 10 times, but 60, 70 or 100 - a super-orgasm. In this documentary, a group of these women volunteer for a series of tests in a scientific investigation to reveal the truth behind the super-orgasm. The aim is to measure and record the phenomenon for the first time, finding out how the brain and the body react before, during and after, and asking if these women are wired differently to other people. And if not, is it something that could be learned by anyone?

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sandra Dee's, a seafood and barbecue restaurant in desperate need of a renovation in Sacramento, California. His team have just 24 hours to refurbish the establishment, and Gordon creates an exciting new menu by incorporating existing dishes and elevating them to a higher standard. Will returning customers be impressed by the changes?

Day two sees Marcus and Miguel lay on a Malaysian feast, but Sean's teasing of his girlfriend steals the limelight. Finally it's Geoff and Jill's chance to shine with their hippy vibe and Indian themed menu, so it's a battle of the curries. programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at 20 years of finding homes for people who were arriving in, or returning to, the UK. British-born Paul and his American wife Lauren are moving to the UK from Holland. However, they have never viewed a house here and it proves a shock. Olivier and Cheyvonne cannot believe how much the housing market has changed since they left for France, while Paul and Alissa want an American-style house in Windsor, but the limited space available for their money proves a disappointment

programme.desc: The documentary focusing on various aspects of the legal system follows three of Hampshire's prolific repeat offenders, who are all trying to reform their lives after their release from prison. They are supported by probation and police, doing the best they can, but large caseloads and a lack of resources leave officers overwhelmed - and when it's the job of probation to manage and assess the risk posed by offenders in the community, the consequences of mistakes can be disastrous

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Week By the Sea
programme.desc: The comedian visits seaside resorts around the UK, beginning in Great Yarmouth, where she is joined by Joe Pasquale. Susan starts her tour with a visit to the lord mayor, who sends her off with a list of things to see and do. She takes part in a synchronised swimming performance at Britain's last surviving circus building, takes a ride on a 1930s rollercoaster, and learns the secret of putting words into a stick of rock

programme.title: Great British Gardens with Carol Klein
programme.desc: Carol visits Bressingham Gardens in Norfolk, home of the Bloom dynasty of Norfolk horticulturists who introduced British gardeners to a wealth of plants and devised a revolutionary way to display them. Carol spends time with Adrian Bloom, whose ethos is to introduce the very best examples of plants that will grace even a tiny garden

programme.title: Inside the World's Greatest Hotels
programme.desc: The Ritz ranks high among London's most legendary hotels, one that has been synonymous with luxury and wealth, as well as being a bastion of privilege for over 100 years. Situated on Piccadilly, just a stone's throw from Buckingham Palace, it has been a home from home for the royal family since the reign of Edward VII. The Ritz has redefined service in luxury hotels, introducing the now-universal concept of the customer always being right. This programme examines its heritage, its famous afternoon teas and the scandalous history of its founder, Cesar Ritz

programme.title: Die Hard 4.0 (2007)
programme.desc: Tough New York cop John McClane is sent to escort a computer hacker to Washington for questioning. During this routine operation he stumbles on a criminal mastermind's plan to launch a technological terrorist attack on the US, and is forced to enlist the hacker's aid in foiling the evil scheme. Action thriller sequel, with Bruce Willis, Timothy Olyphant, Justin Long and Maggie Q

programme.title: Spectacular Spain with Alex Polizzi
programme.desc: On the Costa Blanca, Alex reveals how thriving port Valencia has much more to offer than just its sandy beaches, sampling horchata, a sweet drink made from tiger nuts, and local speciality paella. She also visits the cathedral, where a small chapel houses an ancient chalice believed by some to be the Holy Grail. Further south is Benidorm, where Alex explores its transformation from a quiet fishing village to a high-rise holiday resort. Finally, she visits the tiny island of Tabarca, only a short boat trip away from the mainland but part of a very different world

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Dan Jones examines Britain's most iconic canals, beginning with the 152-mile Grand Union Canal, which runs up the spine of the country. Constructed at the height of the Industrial Revolution, the task challenged even the greatest engineering minds of the time, including pioneers such as James Brindley, the man behind Britain's first commercial canal, and William Jessop, the person with whom responsibility lay for the building of the main link from Birmingham to London. Travelling along its route, Dan experiences first-hand the innovations behind its construction, including the Blisworth tunnel

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Week By the Sea
programme.desc: The comedian explores Brighton, joined by Zoe Lyons. The tour begins at the famous viewing tower, and takes in the toy museum and a game of volleyball on one of the few areas on sandy beach the famously shingle-covered coastline has. Susan also tries her hand at the games on Brighton Palace Pier and discovers why the town is becoming famous for locally produced wine

programme.title: Bronte's Britain with Gyles Brandreth
programme.desc: Gyles travels to the West Yorkshire moors, a landscape synonymous with the stories of Charlotte, Emily and Anne Bronte, who spent almost their entire lives there. Gyles visits the Parsonage, where the sisters lived and which is now preserved as the Bronte Museum. However, 130 years ago it was where the young Brontes' imaginations flourished. As children, they invented imaginary worlds here, and as adults wrote literary classics such as Jane Eyre, Wuthering Heights and The Tenant of Wildfell Hall

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: The hospital struggles with a huge backlog of untreated patients from the night shift, as staff have a hard time ensuring that everyone is discharged or admitted within four hours of arrival. An 85-year-old motorist is treated for a severe head injury after skidding on ice, her car ending up on its roof. A woman who tripped while shopping fears that a broken ankle will jeopardise her plans to go on a cruise

programme.title: 999: Critical Condition
programme.desc: Thirty-eight-year-old Lesley is rushed in when her heart stops while she is driving to work, leaving Dr Morris battle to resuscitate her and investigate why a normally fit and well woman with no known medical problems would suffer such an attack. Abigail, aged 20, is treated for a painful neck injury caused by a riding fall, and 28-year-old Laura is brought in having a severe asthma attack. It is clear to Dr Fawcett she is deteriorating fast and that her heart could stop at any moment

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Writer and historian Dan Jones navigates Britain's longest canal to be built by just one company, the Leeds and Liverpool Canal. Built to link the economically booming counties of Yorkshire and Lancashire, this 19th-century superhighway was experimental for the time, and made Liverpool the busiest port in the world. In Burnley, the cotton capital of the 19th century, Dan visits the world's only steam-powered cotton mill, where enough cloth was woven each year to reach to the moon and back two-and-a-half times

programme.title: Inside Tesco: 24/7
programme.desc: The retailer's transformation from a cut-price upstart to market leader. After the death of its founder Jack Cohen in 1979, the company was in the middle of a high-risk transition and an outside takeover was a real danger. Ian MacLaurin, Jack Cohen's first management trainee, was chief executive at the time. His vision for the company was very different from Jack's pile-it-high, sell-it cheap approach. He set out to transform the company and compete with the number one retailer Sainsbury's - something that was achieved in 1995

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Week By the Sea
programme.desc: The comedian continues her trip around the UK visiting seaside resorts. In the third episode, Susan reaches her next destination, St Ives. Here, she meets Gyles Brandreth, who discusses his personal connection to the famous resort

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
