programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A patient brought into Hull Royal Infirmary by police claims to have swallowed drugs, but he refuses to co-operate with staff, putting them under further pressure. In resus, a patient's life hangs in the balance as the team assess the extent of a serious brain bleed, which could be life-altering, while a 17-year-old arrives after injuring his ankle at a skate park

programme.title: Bridget Jones's Diary (2001)
programme.desc: A single woman begins a new year with resolutions to find love, quit her bad habits and take a step up the career ladder. Her plans crumble as she tries to deal with her mother's infidelity, get over a passionate fling with her boss and come to terms with her feelings for an uptight lawyer. Romantic comedy based on Helen Fielding's novel, starring Renee Zellweger, Hugh Grant and Colin Firth

programme.title: Spectacular Spain with Alex Polizzi
programme.desc: The hotelier stays at the feted Marbella Beach Club, where she meets the man who has played host to A-list jetsetters for decades and has many interesting tales to tell. Moving on to the wild frontier terrain of Western Andalucia, an area often overlooked by tourists, Alex arrives in the quintessentially Spanish town of Jerez, the home of sherry, and learns how this rich drink is produced while admiring the amazing dancing steps of the local horses. Finally, she arrives in the vibrant city of Seville, capital of Andalucia, which she explores on bicycle

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Writer and historian Dan Jones examines the creation of the picturesque Kennet and Avon Canal, which was born from a murky pool of dirty deals and demanding landowners. It came into being thanks to the genius of young engineer John Rennie, whose inspiration and innovations created a waterway that linked Bristol to London. Dan discovers how Rennie plotted the canal's route using the most basic of surveying instruments and marvels at the engineering innovations that made it all possible, such as the water-powered pumping station at Claverton

programme.title: House Busters
programme.desc: Janet Ellis and her team of new-age practitioners suggest ways in which changes to homes could positively affect the lives of their owners

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice visits Kent to offer advice to a Maidstone homeowner looking to relocate after his girlfriend moved out and took most of the furniture with her

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Samson gets lost after he flees from a fog warning that he thinks is the Earl's fireworks display

programme.title: Paw Patrol
programme.desc: The pups must try to rescue Cap'n Turbot and Francois' grandma Tilly after she rides off on a dolphin

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: A series of half hour programmes aimed at reflecting the interests, needs and aspirations of the sign language community

programme.title: His Girl Friday (1940)
programme.desc: A conniving newspaper editor is horrified to learn his ex-wife - who is also happens to be his best reporter - is planning to remarry. Determined to keep her on his staff, he comes up with the one thing he knows she cannot resist - a big story. Meanwhile, he arranges it so that her fiance has a run of misfortune. Comedy, starring Cary Grant, Rosalind Russell and Ralph Bellamy

programme.title: The Million Pound Note (1954)
programme.desc: An eccentric multimillionaire bets his equally rich brother that if a man is known to be wealthy, he can obtain everything he needs on credit. To put this theory to the test, they give a £1million note to a penniless seaman, but instruct him never to spend it. Comedy adapted from a story by Mark Twain, starring Gregory Peck, Jane Griffiths, Joyce Grenfell and Wilfrid Hyde White

programme.title: Santa Fe Passage (1955)
programme.desc: A wagon scout accused of collusion with Apaches is given a chance to redeem himself when he is hired to transport a shipment of arms to Santa Fe. The owner of the valuable freight happens to be a mixed-race woman and at first he shows her only disrespect, but has to overcome his prejudice when he finds himself falling for her. Western adventure, with John Payne, Faith Domergue and Slim Pickens

programme.title: Shenandoah (1965)
programme.desc: A Virginian farmer is left to raise seven children after his wife dies. He tries to keep his family out of the American Civil War, but when Union troops abduct his son, and his son-in-law enlists with the Confederates, he is unable to maintain his neutral stance and is dragged into the conflict. Western, starring James Stewart, Doug McClure, Rosemary Forsyth, Katharine Ross and Patrick Wayne

programme.title: GI Joe: The Rise of Cobra (2009)
programme.desc: When a powerful experimental weapon is snatched by a mysterious terrorist organisation, two of the soldiers guarding it are recruited into a top-secret military strike force. As they go in search of the stolen technology, they uncover a plot by its creator to take over the world. Action adventure, with Channing Tatum, Sienna Miller and Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: The Last Stand (2013)
programme.desc: Disgraced narcotics cop Ray Owens retires into a quiet life as sheriff of a small town on the Mexican border. He gets the chance to prove his worth as a law enforcer when a drug lord escapes from prison and tries to flee the US - with his escape route taking him through Ray's jurisdiction. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Johnny Knoxville and Forest Whitaker

programme.title: American Ultra (2015)
programme.desc: Small-town store clerk and unmotivated stoner Mike is enjoying his life with girlfriend Phoebe when his world is suddenly turned upside down. He is unaware he is actually a highly trained, lethal government sleeper agent who has been targeted for assassination, forcing him to summon his inner action-hero in order to survive. Action comedy, starring Jesse Eisenberg and Kristen Stewart

programme.title: And Then We Danced (2019)
programme.desc: Merab and Irakli dance for the National Georgian Ensemble in Tbilisi. They begin a relationship, but are forced to keep it a secret because of the rigidly macho world in which they work. Drama, starring Levan Gelbakhiani and Bachi Valishvili. In Georgian with English subtitles

programme.title: Apache Drums (1951)
programme.desc: A gunslinging gambler is thrown out of an isolated frontier town for his improper behaviour. He returns to warn the unsuspecting settlers of an impending attack by a band of Apaches fighting to regain possession of their lost territory - but struggles to convince them of the danger. Western, starring Stephen McNally, Willard Parker, Coleen Gray and Arthur Shields

programme.title: The Gentle Sex (1943)
programme.desc: Seven women from vastly different backgrounds are brought together as volunteers in the Auxiliary Territorial Service during the Second World War. Despite their differences they become firm friends during training and on assignment driving military transport and operating anti-aircraft guns. Drama, starring Rosamund John, Joan Greenwood, Lilli Palmer, John Justin and Joan Gates

programme.title: This Happy Breed (1944)
programme.desc: A working-class family moves into a new suburban home after the father returns from service in the First World War. However, the couple and their children struggle to adjust to life higher up the social ladder. David Lean's drama, adapted by Noel Coward from his play, starring Robert Newton, Celia Johnson, Robert Stanley Holloway and John Mills

programme.title: Tobruk (1967)
programme.desc: Jewish resistance fighters out to destroy Nazi fuel bunkers during the North African campaign recruit a Canadian major experienced in desert warfare. Risking life and limb, they pose as a German transport detail escorting British prisoners, but it becomes clear that someone in their group is a traitor. Second World War adventure, starring George Peppard, Rock Hudson, Nigel Green and Guy Stockwell

programme.title: Cowboys & Aliens (2011)
programme.desc: A Wild-West town dominated by a cattle baron comes under attack by invaders from another world. It falls to a mysterious stranger with no memory to unite the townsfolk, a gang of outlaws and an Apache tribe, in a bid to fight back against the aliens, and learn the truth about his own past. Sci-fi Western, with Daniel Craig, Harrison Ford, Olivia Wilde and Sam Rockwell

programme.title: Shooter (2007)
programme.desc: A former Marine sniper is coaxed back into action when government officials need his help to prevent an assassination attempt on the US president. However, the intended target is in fact an African archbishop, and the marksman is set up by double-crossing agents to take the fall, forcing him to go on the run as he fights to clear his name. Action thriller, with Mark Wahlberg, Michael Pena and Danny Glover

programme.title: Eagle Eye (2008)
programme.desc: Two strangers find themselves on the run from the authorities when they receive mysterious phone calls informing them their lives are in danger. They are forced to follow the instructions of a woman who is tracking their every move, while at the same time trying to discover who is behind the conspiracy. Thriller, starring Shia LaBeouf, Michelle Monaghan, Rosario Dawson and Michael Chiklis

programme.title: Let's Be Cops (2014)
programme.desc: Two friends go to a fancy-dress party as police officers, and are amazed by how people on the street react to their uniforms. They decide to pose as cops in the hope of enjoying the perks of the job, but soon end up in over their heads with gangsters and corrupt members of the real force. Comedy, starring Jake Johnson and Damon Wayans Jr

programme.title: Waterloo Road (1944)
programme.desc: A soldier serving in the Second World War discovers his wife has betrayed him by having an affair with a petty crook, so he resolves to settle matters and deserts from the army. He returns to a Blitz-ravaged London to confront his rival, but his quest for retribution is interrupted by an air raid. Drama, starring John Mills, Joy Shelton, Alastair Sim and Stewart Granger

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes (1979)
programme.desc: An American heiress befriends an elderly lady on a continental train journey, but wakes one morning to find she has disappeared and the other passengers deny having ever seen the missing woman - leading to suspicions that a conspiracy is afoot. Remake of Hitchcock's 1938 mystery, starring Elliott Gould, Cybill Shepherd, Angela Lansbury, Herbert Lom, Arthur Lowe and Ian Carmichael

programme.title: Two Rode Together (1961)
programme.desc: The desperate relatives of people captured by Comanches many years before put pressure on the US military to bring them home. The army hires a cynical frontier marshal, who agrees to negotiate their release in return for a commission. But the townsfolk are forced to face some grim truths when he returns with just one unrecognisable captive. John Ford's Western, with James Stewart, Richard Widmark and Shirley Jones

programme.title: Beneath the Planet of the Apes (1969)
programme.desc: A second astronaut arrives on the strange simian-dominated world searching for his predecessor. As he journeys into the infamous forbidden zone, he discovers an underground cult of psychic mutants. Meanwhile, a military coup has taken place among the ape society, leading to an inevitable battle. Sci-fi sequel, with James Franciscus, Charlton Heston and Kim Hunter

programme.title: Gods of Egypt (2016)
programme.desc: An ancient Egyptian god is driven into exile when his evil brother murders their father and seizes his throne. He enlists the aid of a human thief in restoring justice, and in return offers to help the mortal to bring his murdered lover back to life. Fantasy adventure, starring Nikolaj Coster-Waldau, Brenton Thwaites, Gerard Butler and Chadwick Boseman

programme.title: Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit (2014)
programme.desc: A former soldier injured in the line of duty takes up a new career as a financial analyst for the CIA. However, his deskbound position changes to a dangerous undercover assignment when he discovers a plot to destroy the US economy. Spy thriller inspired by Tom Clancy's novels, starring Chris Pine, Keira Knightley, Kevin Costner and Kenneth Branagh, who also directed

programme.title: The Handmaiden (2016)
programme.desc: A conman posing as a count hires a petty criminal to help him marry a Japanese heiress by becoming her handmaiden and persuading her to accept his proposal. Psychological thriller based on Sarah Waters' bestselling novel Fingersmith, starring Kim Min-hee, Kim Tae-ri, Ha Jung-woo and Cho Jin-woong. In Korean and English

Coming-of-age drama, starring Glen Berry, Scott Neal, Linda Henry, Meera Syal and Ben Daniels

programme.desc: Santiago hosts a Thanksgiving dinner at her apartment for all her colleagues, but the event risks turning into a disaster, especially when Holt and Peralta have to leave in a hurry to catch a thief who has stolen a large sum of money from the evidence lock-up. Can Boyle save the day?

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta is put in charge of Captain Holt's security after he receives several death threats - a responsibility he not only enjoys, but also abuses as much as he can. Santiago tries to encourage her colleagues to take a Christmas photo, only to be met with a lack of enthusiasm - and Diaz's refusal to smile. US cop comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang become concerned when Sheldon's former admirer, Dr Ramona Nowitzki, resurfaces while Amy is away working at Princeton University. Jim Parsons stars

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard angers the university - and the entire physics community - when he gives an embarrassing interview. Amy and Bernadette bond over having to hide their success from Sheldon and Howard. Ira Flatow and Regina King guest star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon panics when Leonard announces he has to go into hospital for surgery, and the impending divorce of Raj's parents motivates Howard and Bernadette to work on their own marriage. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon bids an emotional goodbye to Fun with Flags as he and Amy record the final instalment of the internet show. Leonard, Howard and Raj clear out the office of a recently deceased professor and take a peek at his research. Guest starring LeVar Burton

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: At Halloween, Barry and Erica persuade Adam not to go trick-or-treating but to take his girlfriend Dana to a haunted house instead. Beverly attempts to teach Adam a lesson about the dangers of the spooky celebration, but her plan backfires. Dustin Ybarra guest stars

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry badgers Murray into getting him a puppy, but he is upset when the dog takes more of a liking to his father than to him. Beverly wants to join in with Erica and Lainey's pyjama party, but when the girls exclude her from the gathering she soon finds a way to eavesdrop on them

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre and Rainbow want to send Kyra to Valley Glen Prep, but they become infuriated when the school treats them like a charity case. Meanwhile, Andre Jr is keen to get a job as Josh's assistant at Stevens & Lido

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Kyra's estranged father turns up and reveals he wants to take her home to Houston, so Dre and Rainbow try to show him the amazing life they can provide for her

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Boyle asks Peralta to be his best man, but as well as helping out with cake-tasting and choosing invitations, he must also persuade the groom to start standing up to Vivian before the big day. Holt helps Diaz make a sincere apology and Jeffords, Santiago and Gina embark on a tough new diet - but struggle to stick to it

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta enlists Jeffords to help him tackle a cold case that everyone thinks is unsolvable. Meanwhile, Santiago plans a romantic weekend away with her new boyfriend Teddy, and Gina and Diaz tell Boyle one of their best-kept secrets. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg and Melissa Fumero

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon petitions Wil Wheaton to allow him to appear on the new Professor Proton show, so the actor offers a guest appearance to Amy instead. Meanwhile, Penny gets angry when she thinks the female protagonist in Leonard's novel is based on her

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Bernadette's friends each try different tactics to help her go into labour. Howard isn't happy when he finds out that Bernadette has already chosen their son's name ithout his input

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Glamorous solicitor Aisling hosts the third dinner party in Edinburgh and throws a traditional Irish party, with a piper at the door to welcome her guests

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Bridgeport, Connecticut, to visit Cafe Tavolini, which is being neglected by husband and wife managers teetering on the edge of divorce. The estranged couple's two children are struggling to save the business from their parents' disputes, and the visiting chef tries to shock the pair into stopping the restaurant from becoming a casualty of their quarrel

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, including Line of Duty, The Great Celebrity Bake Off for SU2C, The Flight Attendant, Teen First Dates, The Circle, Remarkable Places to Eat, This Morning featuring Matt Hancock, and the News on the Suez Canal

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Programmes under review include James May: Oh Cook, Strictly Come Dancing, The Crown, I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!, Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat, Naked Attraction, and news coverage of Dominic Cummings and Donald Trump

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson hosts the show in which potential dates bare all bit by bit in a bid to prove they are the one. First up is 30-year-old woman Emmy-Lou from Brighton, who has appeared on the show twice before, both times in the pod. Now she is back as the picker, unaware she is in for a big surprise. She is followed by 20-year-old Charlie from Oxford, a forensics student who hopes to find his ideal partner in crime

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional views of TV from September 2016, including Victoria, The Great British Bake Off, Hunted, Penelope Keith's Hidden Villages, Body Fixers and the News

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny hosts a Halloween costume party, which proves a worthy test for the boys' social skills. During the event, Leonard has a run-in with Penny's ex-boyfriend. Kaley Cuoco, Johnny Galecki and Simon Helberg star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny is forced to take Howard's place in the boys' video-game night while he is otherwise engaged with one of her friends. Simon Helberg, Kaley Cuoco and Johnny Galecki star

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Dental nurse Jade, who's 25, is set up on a date with handsome tree surgeon Tom. Meanwhile, Sharran - the UK's only female sumo wrestler - meets David, a former bingo-caller, who has been unlucky in love in the past, and City trader Adam is set up on a date with 28-year-old party girl Leena from Liverpool

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, including Line of Duty, The Great Celebrity Bake Off for SU2C, The Flight Attendant, Teen First Dates, The Circle, Remarkable Places to Eat, This Morning featuring Matt Hancock, and the News on the Suez Canal

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben learns that is it all his fault that Riley does not like celebrating her birthday. He plans a surprise to make it up to her but, in typical fashion, disaster ensues. Meanwhile, Bonnie reveals that she forgot to complete some paperwork and that Ben isn't Emma's sole custodian. Starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben and Danny wear elf suits on their quest to find a particular Christmas gift for Emma, while Riley and Tucker help Bonnie plan a seasonal celebration. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau and Tahj Mowry

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: The interns face their white coat interview with Dr Cox, and Lucy realises she does not stand a chance of passing unless she can give her reasons for wanting to become a doctor. Meanwhile, Denise turns to Turk and Elliot for relationship advice, and Cole reveals Drew's most embarrassing secret. Medical comedy, starring Kerry Bishe

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: Lucy begins to notice everyone at the hospital seems to be involved in a relationship, and tries to re-evaluate her feelings for Cole. Meanwhile, Turk puts his rivalry with Dr Cox to one side for a gravely ill patient, and Denise plays a prank on the security guards. Medical comedy, starring Kerry Bishe

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre and Rainbow want to send Kyra to Valley Glen Prep, but they become infuriated when the school treats them like a charity case. Meanwhile, Andre Jr is keen to get a job as Josh's assistant at Stevens & Lido

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp joins jewellery-maker Rachel Jeffrey to help a soon-to-be-married couple create their own wedding rings, and learns how gold can revive an old picture frame at little cost

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers are surprised when a new heir comes to light on a case they thought was complete, and a story of wartime aviation is revealed. Plus, a woman tries to find out more about her deceased cousin who was a chess champion and playwright

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo head to Wales to see how can create the most profit on items, including an oil dispenser, fire grate, chaise longue and even a catamaran

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien attempt to restore items including an old cheese press, and a 1980s motorbike almost gets the better of Henry and his restorer Guy

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim, Fuzz and the team work on a 1963 Alfa Romeo Giulia Spider that has been kept in a dusty barn in Essex after its owner's plans to restore it were interrupted by the onset of a serious medical condition

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A Scottish couple move to a millionaire's playground, two restaurateurs branch out into weddings and a pair of adrenaline junkies diversify into the B&B business

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Anglesey, where best pals Richard Tuke and Susan Hatch hope to show that Blackthorn Farm B&B, which they own, is better than any of their guests' establishments. However, they find that their close friendship is scrutinised even more than their property

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The White Swan Hotel in Yeadon, West Yorkshire, is the second stop of the competition, and owners Graham and Barbara Yellow hope their basic facilities and affordable prices will please their guests. Spiritual couple Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor get good vibes from their room, Rachel Lonergan views the hosts in a new light and breakfast is well received by the contestants, despite having to pay a supplement fee

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Vegetarians Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor host at the Old Mill House B&B in Bridport, Dorset, and both come under attack at dinner when Malcolm voices his opinions on meat eating. In the morning, a meditation session gets a mixed response, and most of the guests are left cold by the vegetarian buffet breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Rachel and Dan Lonergan welcome their guests to the Brighton Inn, East Sussex, where they hope to offer a luxury, boutique experience. Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor give their room a thorough inspection, before the groups learns the art of building a human pyramid in the afternoon's activity. At breakfast, they discover a lack of vegetarian alternatives, and the hosts suspect game-playing when they are shocked by some negative feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together to confront their rivals and find out how much they have been paid. Richard and Susan are keen to tackle the bathroom issues of Barbara and Graham, who bask in the comments from Malcolm and Fiona. The Old Mill House proprietors defend their vegetarian breakfast routine, but serve up snobby feedback to Rachel and Dan from the Brighton Inn

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Musicians Justine Cormack and Marc Taddei swap their Auckland home for the mountains of Central Otago. They risk all by adapting an American design for the Southern Hemisphere

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A dachshund is brought in by owners Kelly and Daniel to see if anything can be done to repair a deformity in its foot, and Noel Fitzpatrick gives the one-year-old a lesson in doggy discipline along the way. A 10-month-old labrador needs a hip replacement before it can begin training as a medical-alert dog to help its owner with her rare health condition. An eight-year-old shih-tzu has the most extraordinary walk the vet has ever seen, but the operation to fix the dog's spine will be extremely risky, presenting the owners with an agonising decision

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Fresh from the success of hosting a three-day wedding at the chateau, Dick and Angel make business plans for the grounds, intending to charge people to fish in the moat and forage in the woods. Angel starts work on a second guest suite, but also wants to install an elevator, which pushes Dick's engineering skills to the limit

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows software executive Andy and garden designer Nicki, who are planning to build a 'floating' house on the Thames, which would be the first of its kind in the UK. Their architect has come up with an ambitious, experimental design that would see the whole property magically rise up with the flood waters. However, no one knows whether it will work, and the project soon becomes bogged down in the logistical challenges of bringing to fruition such a complex structure on a small island that is only accessible by a narrow footbridge

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows more of Britain's most ambitious self-building projects, as intrepid individuals attempt to design and construct the home of their dreams. In this first edition, Kevin meets former army captain Justin, who has spent £1.8million on an abandoned neo-Gothic cemetery keeper's lodge and adjoining ex-council toilet block in south-west London. Justin's vision is to restore the lodge but demolish the ugly toilet blocks and replace them with an enormous luxury ground-floor extension, with an added moat and a giant basement with a swimming pool - all just metres from the nearest grave

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a West Sussex couple planning to build a house on the back of an old trailer, while in Wiltshire, a single mother plans to transform an old military helicopter, and a water tower undergoes a surprising conversion in Warwickshire. George's tour of Spain continues in northern Andalusia, where he visits a cave-style retreat quarried deep into the mountains

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about life inside an A&E department, following patients treated in the same 24-hour period at St George's in south-west London. Fourteen-year-old Eduard is rushed in after falling from a swing and fracturing his ankle, while 62-year-old Rosina is treated having broken her wrist on the dancefloor at a family party. Paula is in Resus having been unable to take in food through her feeding tube and it is not only her health that is in danger, but also that of her unborn baby

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with Lee Mack and Victoria Coren Mitchell taking on Alan Carr and Dane Baptiste. Sam Simmons joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows more of Britain's most ambitious self-building projects, as intrepid individuals attempt to design and construct the home of their dreams. In this first edition, Kevin meets former army captain Justin, who has spent £1.8million on an abandoned neo-Gothic cemetery keeper's lodge and adjoining ex-council toilet block in south-west London. Justin's vision is to restore the lodge but demolish the ugly toilet blocks and replace them with an enormous luxury ground-floor extension, with an added moat and a giant basement with a swimming pool - all just metres from the nearest grave

Fourteen-year-old Eduard is rushed in after falling from a swing and fracturing his ankle, while 62-year-old Rosina is treated having broken her wrist on the dancefloor at a family party. Paula is in Resus having been unable to take in food through her feeding tube and it is not only her health that is in danger, but also that of her unborn baby

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits the largest wholesale food market in the world in Paris and discovers why British cheese sales are soaring across Europe. Matt Tebbutt travels to one of the largest ham producers in Italy and meets a pig farmer who tells him about a delicate problem that all pork producers face, while Kate Quilton investigates why some limes are so much juicier than others

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enters the single-cake class at the Devon County Show, and hopes to win over the judges with her own Caribbean-inspired recipe. However, it is a fellow competitor's beetroot cake that ends up raising their eyebrows

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers search for the living relatives of a man whose father was a magician, and share the story of how they were able to tell family members about an aunt they did not know they had. Plus, the hunters reveal more details of unclaimed estates

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo restore and upcycle 2CV headlamps, a children's rocking horse, a Victorian streetlamp and a high chair

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien explore a fairground, and collect a bicycle made for two, a ship's search lamp and a lambing chair

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to Hampshire to repair a 1982 VW Golf GTi Mk1 that has been kept in a garage since a road accident in 2001. However, they both have different ideas about how the finished car should look when it is returned to its owner

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A Scottish couple welcome guests to their Spanish villa, two French B&B owners are pushed to their limits, and a couple reveal plans to take their kite school business to Kenya

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Charing, Kent, where landlady Natalie Palmer hopes to show that her B&B, The Oak, is better than any of her guests' establishments. Everything starts off well when a couple are delighted to have enough room for their yoga mats, but the rest of the party are unimpressed with the extractor fans

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants visit the White Lady in Worstead, Norfolk, on their second night, where proprietor Dennis Gilligan tells his guests about a spooky presence that resides there. The guests get competitive in a round of clay shooting, and debate whether they believe in the existence of ghosts

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Cranleigh House B&B in Combe Martin, Devon, where former couple Katherine Armitage and Stewart Johnston offer alternative accommodation for those who enjoy yoga and are seeking spiritual enlightenment. They hope the group will send out energy for world peace and healing, but Mike and Denise question whether the whole thing is a joke

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike and Denise Mitchell welcome the contestants to Beaches B&B in Paignton, Devon, which only opened for business a few weeks ago. Fellow competitor Katherine puts the owners through their paces at breakfast when she asks for something off the menu, and the hosts are pleasantly surprised at feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together for one last time to discover what they have been paid, and the identity of the winner. The Oak owner Natalie wants to know why some of her guests were not enthused by her extractor fans, but she herself receives aggression from Dennis over her criticism of the White Lady. Katherine and Stuart defend their vegetarian breakfast, and Mike and Denise deal with negative comments about their cereal selection

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: David and Tracey share a passion for architecture but with diverging taste. Their home in Waipu Cove results in a clash of wills and a battle over the budget and infinity pool

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A dachshund is brought in by owners Kelly and Daniel to see if anything can be done to repair a deformity in its foot, and Noel Fitzpatrick gives the one-year-old a lesson in doggy discipline along the way. A 10-month-old labrador needs a hip replacement before it can begin training as a medical-alert dog to help its owner with her rare health condition. An eight-year-old shih-tzu has the most extraordinary walk the vet has ever seen, but the operation to fix the dog's spine will be extremely risky, presenting the owners with an agonising decision

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Two-and-a-half years have passed since Dick and Angel Strawbridge swapped their flat in Southend for a 19th-century chateau in France costing only £280,000, and in that time, the pair have worked to transform the derelict property into a fairytale castle. However, around half of the 45 rooms are yet to receive adequate attention, the couple's fledgling events business demands attention, and the outbuildings are still in need of conversion

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits a memorable restoration project in the remote Creuse region of central France. Back in 2003, when Denise Daniel and Doug Ibbs gave up everything in the UK to start a new life abroad, they couldn't have known what was ahead of them. Falling in love with a dilapidated manor house with just four crumbling walls and half a roof, they embarked on a mission to transform it - working day and night, doing everything themselves, year after year - until slowly but surely they created a magnificent edifice out of the ruins they bought off the internet. On the 70th anniversary of the destruction of the original 19th-century building by the German army, the house finally took its finished form

programme.title: The Windsors: Inside the Royal Dynasty
programme.desc: The second episode explores the coronation of George VI in the aftermath of the succession crisis, and the efforts of the uncertain new monarch to rebuild the prestige of a troubled monarchy as the nation faced the prospect of being drawn into the Second World War

programme.title: The Windsors: Secrets of the Royal Tours
programme.desc: Documentary examining official tours and state visits by the royal family, exploring the history of the symbolic locations on the touring map, beginning by capturing the celebration and optimism after the end of the Second World War

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-three-year-old Mohammad, who has motor neurone disease, is rushed to St George's with severe sepsis and doctors work to get the infection under control, while David has fallen 20 feet from a billboard and the trauma team is concerned that the 47-year-old has suffered life-changing injuries. Retired journalist Tom is in the urgent care centre after knocking his arm and his wife Eileen reveals how their plans have been turned upside down due to an unexpected bombshell six weeks previously

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with Johnny Vegas and Rhod Gilbert taking on Joe Wilkinson and Roisin Conaty. Tom Allen joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: The Windsors: Inside the Royal Dynasty
programme.desc: The second episode explores the coronation of George VI in the aftermath of the succession crisis, and the efforts of the uncertain new monarch to rebuild the prestige of a troubled monarchy as the nation faced the prospect of being drawn into the Second World War

programme.title: The Windsors: Secrets of the Royal Tours
programme.desc: Documentary examining official tours and state visits by the royal family, exploring the history of the symbolic locations on the touring map, beginning by capturing the celebration and optimism after the end of the Second World War

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt visits the town of Tequila on the trail of Mexico's national drink as he tries to find out where the idea of a worm in the bottle came from, and Jimmy Doherty learns how Poles transform carp into a much-loved national delicacy. Kate Quilton visits Spain to explore the difference between tangerines, mandarins, satsumas and clementines

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp demonstrates how to create handmade and personalised gifts. She learns about sugarcraft and perfume-making, and also receives a lesson in the art of designing Victorian-style silhouettes

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The story of a family divided by agricultural hardship who ended up living on opposite sides of the world, and as the team races to find the heirs to a recently advertised unclaimed estate, a surprise connection is made in Malta

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit landed gent Sir Benjamin Slade as they search for more upcycling prospects. Simon converts a gilded picture frame into a flat-screen TV surround, while Henry refurbishes a club fender fire surround and turns a quick profit on a Fordson tractor

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find, fix and flog unwanted possessions on behalf of their owners. Henry and Simon realise profit potential in an old crop sprayer, a cast iron fire guard and some old wooden workshop clamps

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim, Fuzz and the team must race against the clock when they step in and restore high end car, a 1967 Lancia Flavia coupe, that has been hidden in a garage in Rochdale for 30 years

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: The owners of a Spanish kite school debate moving their business to Kenya, a power cut causes problems at a busy B&B and two French holiday hosts are snowed under with jobs to do

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to the Old Times Guesthouse in Ipswich, where mother-and-daughter duo Linda and Kelly Langdown hope a go-karting session will get the contest off to a flying start. However, a lack of breakfast options and corner-cutting on the guests' orders do not go down very well

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests visit the Flower Pot in Sunbury-on-Thames on their second night, where owner Simon Bailey and French manager Pierre Cudon provide stylish continental-inspired accommodation, but the hosts are left puzzled by some of the feedback they receive the next morning

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the family-run Dolphin Hotel in Exmouth, Devon, which has been a life's work for hotelier Jo Knowles. On arrival, Pat and Nigel find a suspicious stain, before the guests put on their war faces for a game of paintball. Jo takes everyone to a floating restaurant for dinner, and the next morning she presents a huge spread for breakfast - but her diners are suspicious that it has been laid on specially for the competition

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants stay at the Mariners Beachside B&B in Seaton, Devon, but are disappointed by the cleanliness of the property. They take part in a town-crying competition during the afternoon, and host Nigel feels under pressure to deliver his award-winning breakfast the next morning. Come feedback time, the owners discover not all their guests share the same opinion

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the final time to find out what they have been paid, and confront their rivals. Simon Bailey and Pierre Cudon from the Flower Pot feel the feedback left by Nigel and Pat Booth is a step too far, while the Mariners B&B owners hit back at the men's opinion of their establishment's cleanliness. Linda and Kelly Langdown of the Old Times Guest House question the rating of their facilities, while the Dolphin Hotel's Jo Knowles has a confession to make regarding her hosting

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Craig Jarvis's tiny 65-square-metre home overlooking Taylor's Mistake has been six years in the making - Grand Designs New Zealand's longest running house build. Chris Moller finds out whether it is finally finished

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick and the team deal with two emergency cases in this edition. Lara the cat went missing after jumping into a removal van, but escaped, only to be hit by a car and then attacked by a fox. One of its rear legs is shattered and the vet has to perform reconstructive surgery to rebuild it. Chief, an eight-year-old Northern Inuit, dislocated its hip while chasing a fox. Noel thinks it needs hip-replacement surgery, but owner Nigel can't afford to pay for the operation

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick gets to work on the walled garden in the spring and buys a mini tractor for the job - but it may not fit through the gate. Angel designs another suite, with black walls and mosaic mirrors, and decorates the entire dining room with blossoms so a guest can make a special wedding proposal. The couple also consider the possibility of truffle farming

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits a Japanese-Welsh fusion home in the Wye Valley, completely transformed from a damp old forester's house in 2013. Tamayo Hussey had missed Japan ever since she moved to the UK 15 years earlier with husband Nigel, so to stave off the homesickness they decided to turn the property into a one-of-a-kind home, complete with roof bath, tatami room and sliding paper walls. Kevin finds out how this somewhat improvised building weathered one of the wettest winters on record and if it has brought the calm and tranquillity Tamayo remembers from her childhood in the Far East

programme.title: Antiques Hunters on Tour
programme.desc: Documentary following antiques dealer and French polisher Rob Cain as he visits France in search of hidden treasures to restore and sell back home in Yorkshire

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old lung cancer patient Marion is rushed to St George's with suspected sepsis. She was discharged from hospital the previous day after having an operation to remove liquid from her lung, but has deteriorated overnight. Eighty-three-year-old Roy is brought into resus after falling off his roof at home, and doctors are concerned that he may have sustained internal injuries, while, five-year-old Laila has come to paediatrics with her mum Clarissa after cutting her head open at school

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and numbers-quiz, inviting Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan to take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson. John Cooper Clarke joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Birmingham, paramedics Adi Ahmed and Mark McDonagh rush to a 10-year-old boy who has had a major epileptic fit. At the same time in Coventry, an elderly woman with heart and lung failure is struggling to breathe but refuses to go to hospital for treatment. Plus, in Warwick, a crew is called to an elderly man who has extreme anxiety and has called emergency services 14 times

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old lung cancer patient Marion is rushed to St George's with suspected sepsis. She was discharged from hospital the previous day after having an operation to remove liquid from her lung, but has deteriorated overnight. programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: It was believed that part of the Nazi party was involved and influenced in all manner of occult beliefs from their uniforms to their huge rallies

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A vast new island in the seas off Dubai, which has been created to house the world's biggest observation wheel - twice the size of the London Eye

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the next leg of his journey from London Bridge to Hastings, exploring the lives of Victorian hop-pickers along the way. He also examines the history of Maidstone's paper industry, and discovers the role railways played in transforming cricket into a national sport

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through Kent. He starts in Canterbury, where he finds out how the city's cathedral was saved from the Second World War air raids, then goes whelk fishing in Whitstable and explores the origins of a seaside swim in Margate

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team of antiques experts visit Erddig, north Wales. On a scorching hot day, the crowds come out, bringing with them items including a dazzling emerald and diamond ring worn by the owner's grandmother on the Titanic, a Welsh love spoon carved in 1859 by a young man for his sweetheart and an avid fan's collection of James Bond props

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Unusual rail networks designed to traverse water, including the Indian Ocean, the earthquake zones of the Bosphorus Strait and the sea cliffs of the Italian Riviera

programme.title: Ganges
programme.desc: How the Gangetic Plains, once a wilderness teeming with wildlife, have been transformed into one of the most densely populated places on Earth - and how the region's tigers, lions, cheetahs and other species have been affected by human encroachment on their habitat

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough profiles the tree-dwellers of the animal kingdom, as primates, bats and squirrels perform complicated manoeuvres in the world's forests. Whether aided by sharp claws, webbed feet or gripping tails, these animals play a complicated game of survival with predators lurking below, ready to make a meal of those who fail to stay aloft

programme.title: The Day When
programme.desc: The United States' entry into the Second World War following the attack on Pearl Harbor, focusing on Roosevelt's decision to engage German forces as well as Japanese

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: How the First World War transformed life on the home front, from a greater number of women in the workplace to increased government interference in everyday life

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team of antiques experts visit Erddig, north Wales. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.desc: On January 22nd 1944, two divisions land at the Italian port of Anzio in one of the easiest Allied amphibious assaults of the war

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: In Poland lies a forgotten Nazi complex which could have sheltered up to 27,244 people from Allied bombs, the purpose of which largely remains a mystery

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: How the SAS War Crimes Investigation Team tried to pinpoint the location of Gestapo officers who were wanted for a host of atrocities

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Concrete channels hidden in a mountaintop forest, a collapsing bridge, a marooned military base and the upright rusting carcass of ship

programme.title: Royals on the Frontline

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Cutting-edge investigations at the Bermuda Triangle might confirm the newest theories of what really goes on in this deadly stretch of water

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Whistler on the Canadian Pacific seaboard, a remote mountain town whose creation was made possible with record-breaking engineering equipment, helping establish one of the best ski resorts in the world

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Nato's Airborne Warning and Control System, known as Awac, a cutting-edge aircraft with a huge radar dome mounted on its roof that enables pilots to spot hostile intrusion as they protect state borders of the countries in the Nato alliance. One of the most complex surveillance aircrafts in the world, it is able to seek out, locate, and defend against threats - no matter how fast moving or well hidden

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: The drive to make trains faster, from supersonic rocket sleds to engines that seem to fly above their guideways

programme.desc: The final leg of the river's journey sees it join its tributary the Brahmaputra. As the waters merge, they overspill their banks to fan out into hundreds of smaller rivulets and create the world's largest delta. The programme reveals how people and wildlife survive the inhospitable habitat, perhaps the last place on Earth where the Bengal tiger is still king

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at vibrantly coloured monkeys in Venezuela, Costa Rica and West Africa. He encounters the red howler, which boasts one of the loudest calls in the wild, a capuchin that uses a tree as an anvil, the guenon, which communicates via facial expression, and the ground-dwelling gelada baboon

programme.title: The Day When
programme.desc: The historical documentary explores how Stalin managed to seize a large chunk of Europe in the wake of the Second World War - to the chagrin of Churchill and Roosevelt

programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Crathes Castle, Aberdeenshire, where finds brought in to be valued include a collection of signed Harry Potter books, ornate silver from Iona, a German art deco figurine, an intricate Italian bracelet, and a baseball cap with an unusual story to tell

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a journey from Ayr to Skye. He begins by visiting the home town of poet Robert Burns and finds out how to make haggis. He then discovers the way in which the railway transformed the game of golf in Prestwick, and uncovers the story of the great Victorian tartan hoax in Paisley

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the second leg of his journey along the west coast of Scotland from Ayr to Skye, stopping off to explore the Dumbarton shipyards that were responsible for the construction of the famous Cutty Sark. He also goes hunting for gold in the mountains, and visits Loch Lomond, one of Queen Victoria's favourite haunts

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo tunes into the music of the mills and collieries of Victorian England by joining a brass band in Honley, West Yorkshire, before stopping off in Holmfirth to learn about a tragedy that led to a tourist boom in the town. The presenter then crosses into Derbyshire to pay homage to railway engineer George Stephenson at his resting place in Chesterfield, before concluding his journey at Chatsworth House, which was one of the first stately homes to welcome visitors by rail

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey to Leeds, beginning at London's Euston station. He finds out what happened to the once proud Euston Arch and heads to Camden to see how goods were transported by rail, road and canal. He reflects on the Harrow rail disaster of 1952 and visits a country estate in Tring, Hertfordshire, before alighting at Cheddington in Buckinghamshire, which is close to the scene of 1963's Great Train Robbery

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: An impressive collection of five iconic cars arrives at Thornton le Dale, including a 1967 Crayford Corsair and a 1966 Ford Zodiac which emerge from a garage in Selby

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Nothing screams 1970s more than a Mk3 Ford Cortina 2000E. Derek Mathewson heads to Brigg to value a 1974 example with classic Coke bottle styling and a sweet Pinto engine

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates are shocked to discover there is a thief in their midst, so Fletcher decides to stage a mock trial to bring the culprit to justice with the help of Slade's resident legal consultant Judge Rawley. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale and Maurice Denham

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates decide on a wager as the parole board gathers, with Blanco odds-on favourite for release. However, the old lag turns down his freedom and leaves Fletcher in danger of losing a small fortune. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: Royals on the Frontline

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: The Vatican is home to classified files that could reveal top-secret ties to the Nazis, the Mafia, and extraterrestrials

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A floating bridge in Seattle, one of the longest such structures in the world, intended to connect the growing suburb of Bellevue with the high-tech downtown heart of the city. Plus, Albert Zwueste is on the Dutch Caribbean island of Curacao exploring one of the world's oldest surviving floating bridges, which helped inspire and inform the Seattle project

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Maritime engineering in recent years has seen ever bigger ships and sea-based platforms constructed. On occasion, they may need moving, which is the purpose of the Boka Vanguard, the planet's largest heavy lift ship, capable of sinking itself, floating under its cargo and then lifting it up to be moved anywhere on the planet with sea. This extraordinary work of engineering is capable of carrying the Eiffel Tower and the Statue of Liberty at the same time

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a journey from Ayr to Skye. He begins by visiting the home town of poet Robert Burns and finds out how to make haggis. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer seafront in Norfolk. Items brought in to be valued include two pieces of Italian jewellery, a Chinese vase, and a silver goblet that survived the Great Fire of London in perfect condition. Philip Mould examines a pencil sketch by LS Lowry to determine if it is real or a fake

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Since their meteoric rise in the 19th Century, railways have been at the forefront of modern engineering. They've conquered dizzying altitudes, wilderness, seas and cities.

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various animals go undercover in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. Spy Pup is immersed in wild dog life to explore their maternal love, and Spy Penguin records the turbulent action of the mating season, as a male Adelie penguin falls victim to a pebble thief that ruins his chances of love for the season. Spy Tortoise and Spy Bushbaby keep watch on chimpanzees, while in perhaps the most moving scenes, Spy Monkey becomes the focus of attention for a troop of concerned langurs who believe the motionless camera-creature has died

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the startling similarities between humans and great apes, meeting an orang-utan able to row boats and wash clothes and a gorilla that can communicate using sign language. He also traces the history of human settlement and considers the future of population control and food production

programme.title: War Above the Trenches

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: The impact of Russia pulling out of the war, only for the US to join soon afterwards, and the events in the aftermath that contributed to the Second World War

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Ayr to Skye and investigates the parallel 'roads' etched in the side of Glen Roy - one of the geological mysteries of the 19th century. programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the experts return to Wightwick Manor near Wolverhampton, where members of the public bring along their treasured antiques and collectibles to hear their stories and find out their worth. The guests offer mixed responses when they look at the menu, but things take a turn for the better when she announces a duel will form part of the evening's entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Massage therapist Lucy Hird hosts the fourth dinner party from the West Midlands, featuring an extensive menu full of Thai specialities. She also gives individual presents to her guests, but her night is compromised by the entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mental-health nurse Lloyd Cast hosts the final dinner party from the West Midlands. Despite appearing to be an extrovert so far, he decides on a chilled theme for the evening to allow his food to take centre stage, before the winner of the £1,000 is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in Los Angeles, where he tries to bring family-owned Mexican restaurant La Serenata back from the brink of disaster. The chef and his team have just 24 hours to update the interior and introduce a more dynamic menu with dishes that give a fresh spin to classic Mexican cuisine. But what will the customers think of the changes?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul prepare for their biggest chateau booking ever as they host American veterans for the 75th anniversary of D-Day. Mariam and Johnno transform their chateau's grand salon by installing reproduction Parquet de Versailles flooring and hand-painted murals. Plus, Karen and Paul aim to transform the old stable and coach house into a space fit for hosting weddings

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: John, a town planner, and Daniel a luxury retail manager, have been together for five years and feel now is the time to find themselves a dream holiday home for their budget of £100,000. Keen to get their teeth into a renovation project, they've chosen the area around Cádiz in southern Spain. Ben Hillman lines up five property options to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas helps house-hunters Shami and Clive find a property on the island of Sicily, suitable for them and their three cats, on a budget of £180,000

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats chocolate Labrador Inca, who has a history of developmental elbow disease and osteoarthritis that has led to her elbow joints becoming worn out. Two-year-old Cavapoo Nala, who has hip dysplasia, and Great Dane Charles, who is unsteady on his feet due to a squashed spinal cord, are also on the patients list. Charles' owner Steve knows only too well that spinal surgery is a hugely serious undertaking, as he himself had multiple surgeries following a bike accident a decade earlier

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge help Kate and Erik restore their 150-year-old sash window and turn their small landing into a beautiful space for all the family that not only looks amazing and brings the past back to life, but saves thousands of pounds. Angel reveals the art of creating repeat patterns on fabric as a unique solution to Jo and family's desire to display their precious vintage family photos in something other than a frame. Dick solves Suzanna and Luke's conundrum of how to turn a treasured wardrobe into a showcase for shoes, by designing ingenious shelving inspired by a visit to the kitchen

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A doctor shares his story of leaving his family and newborn child during lockdown to keep them safe. programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?

In Rotherham, PCs Pete Ellard and Tabitha Rumney have their hands full with a drunk and disorderly couple who are violently resisting arrest

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line

programme.title: Coast vs Country
programme.desc: Kerr Drummond and Kirsty Duffy search Devon and Dorset to find three properties on a single plot of land for an extended family who want to live as neighbours

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The coach party arrives in Albufeira, in the heart of the Portuguese Algarve. Following earlier events, the mood in the camp is low as the group heads off for a competitive sandcastle building contest before a fight for survival at the vote

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps George Robertson from Bedfordshire search for a second home in Majorca's Port de Pollensa, a place he has visited over 30 times

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Helen Broomhead from Cheshire is longing for a bolthole in the picturesque Cinque Terre region of Italy. Jasmine Harman takes her to see five properties for her budget of £100,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A few years ago Kieran Alexander was involved in a serious motorbike accident that has given her, and partner Dave Dwyer, a new outlook on life - that it is for living and there is no time like the present. They've decided to leave their stressful lives in the UK behind and start again in the Azores in the Atlantic. With a budget of £100,000 to fund their new home, Ben Hillman shows them five properties on the main island of Sao Miguel

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Kennels owner Dean Faulkner is keen to impress as he hosts the first dinner party in Cambridge, where his guests are beauty school co-ordinator Kay Greenwood, gardening enthusiast Matt Sullivan, headstrong international development manager Hawa Sydique and the Beatles-obsessed Dave Goodier

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty school co-ordinator Kay Greenwood hosts the second dinner party in Cambridge, hoping her tempura prawns and beef stroganoff will win her the £1,000 prize. One guest is sceptical about her chances of success, but changes his mind when he samples her unique entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Gardening enthusiast Matt Sullivan hosts the third dinner party in Cambridge, where his home-grown English menu of 'posh' fish and chips and rice pudding surprises his guests. Rather than focusing on the cooking, he creates his own school fete and tries to wow everyone with a selection of outdoor games as he vies for the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: International development manager Hawa Sydique hosts the fourth dinner party from Cambridge, and her adventurous menu seems to cause more problems than it is worth. However, she hopes to impress with the 20-piece samba band in her living room, but the other guests are a little overwhelmed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beatles-loving Scouser David Goodier hosts the final dinner party in Cambridge and is determined to impress his guests with a 1960s Merseybeat dress code and courses entitled 'Spicy Beatballs' and 'Tir-Mersey-su'. As the evening draws to a close, the winner of the £1,000 cash prize is revealed

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay tries to improve a family-owned Irish pub in California, stripping out and refurbishing the dining room, creating a simplified menu and giving staff training

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul take on a massive garden project as they clear a lake bed with the aim of connecting it to their current lake, while they also bring their vine house back to life by creating an al fresco dining area. Mariam and Johnno turn on the style as they launch their chateau as an events venue by inviting locals over for a 1930s-themed party. Despite a number of leads the police have never managed to catch Rita's murderer and with a case that's 53 years old, Emilia and David have a challenge tracking down witnesses, other potential victims and a suspect

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Colin and Elspeth from Edinburgh search for a holiday home in Menorca, which they can enjoy well into their retirement with their family

programme.desc: Full-time academic Rosie from Ascot in Berkshire is hoping to find the perfect getaway on the Croatian Island of Hvar. Ben Hillman shows her a selection within a budget of £80,000

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five amateur cooks from Wakefield, West Yorkshire, take turns trying to throw the perfect dinner party. programme.desc: IT expert and disco fanatic Joel Rowbottom throws the second of the week's parties in Wakefield, West Yorkshire, hoping to impress his guests with an exciting mixture of food and dance. programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sandra Dee's, a seafood and barbecue restaurant in desperate need of a renovation in Sacramento, California. programme.title: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: The documentary focusing on various aspects of the legal system follows three of Hampshire's prolific repeat offenders, who are all trying to reform their lives after their release from prison. They are supported by probation and police, doing the best they can, but large caseloads and a lack of resources leave officers overwhelmed - and when it's the job of probation to manage and assess the risk posed by offenders in the community, the consequences of mistakes can be disastrous

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Mandy and Christian's Pride parade finally arrives, but what begins as a stressful day only gets worse when the generator powering the speakers packs up. Can the duo turn things around or will the the revellers be expected to dance to a silent soundtrack? Meanwhile, cockney limo driver Johnny Diamond books two big jobs on the same night, and Bury couple Andy and Sue settle in to their static caravan in the Spanish countryside

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Colin and Lizzy Whiting, who find themselves in the wealthiest 10 percent of Britain, swap their five-bedroom house near Newquay to spend a week living in a two-bed property where Sarah and Ross Timmins live. The Timmins on the other hand, are among the poorest 10 percent in the country, and survive on a tenth of the weekly budget the Withings have

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Cleaning duo Jon and Brennan help illusionist James in Dorset, who has neglected his career, his life, and his home following the recent loss of his parents

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: It is estimated that more than a million vehicles in Britain are fitted with cloned number plates as criminals who switch them to pass by police cameras undetected. On the outskirts of Bradford, a suspected stolen car using cloned plates crashes after running a red light, leaving the occupants of another car with serious injuries, leaving officers to deal with the aftermath, while a manhunt is launched for the offenders

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The police in Bradford pursue a team of suspected car thieves, travelling at speeds of 80mph. North Yorkshire's Road Crime Team track down a stolen Land Rover Defender, but when a suspect makes a run for it into nearby woods, the officers are forced to give chase with nothing but a torch to help them catch their man

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Duggie and Mark show that they are as quick on their feet as they are behind the wheel when a serial offender tries to give them the runaround. A high-speed pursuit comes to a painful finish, PC Dan Myerscough loses patience with a silver-tongued drunk, and officers Martin and Hayley are called out to a 4x4 that has come a cropper due to treacherous ice

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Angus and Sean resort to smashing down a door in a desperate attempt to save a man's life, and Kev and Colin spot a car being driven in the wrong direction down a motorway. Darren encounters a pizza delivery man who is under the influence of alcohol, while out on patrol, Hayley observes the damage that can be caused by Cumbria's treacherously icy roads

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers patrol during Black Eye Friday - one of Cumbria's most alcohol-fuelled nights of the year, and help a woman trapped inside a burning car. Meanwhile, a boy racer is made to call his mother after being caught misbehaving, and a motorway pursuit is brought to an abrupt end

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall meets three more troublesome pets, encountering rampaging pug cross Dolly Parton, overzealous guard dog Ernie, and bullying top dog Blue

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Though Levi is given the all-clear by the hospital after the stage collapse, Sheila and Kyle remain deeply concerned. Yashvi and Ned realise their relationship needs a fresh start

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Christian gets caught up in treating a sky-diver's injuries during his foray to the airfield. Jasmine's first day as head nurse at the ICU does not go according to plan

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Jean Robinson and Daithi Elliffe seek the help of architect Dermot Bannon to turn a listed 19th-century former schoolhouse at Kilternan, which has scenic views of the Dublin mountains, into their ideal family home. The property has been virtually disused for the past 30 years and is dank and dilapidated, with rotting floors, and the new homeowners are seeking a comfortable and bright dwelling with a modern layout. However, despite a generous budget of 360,000 euros, the constraints of a protected structure, a tight schedule and hidden costs begin to take their toll on the project

programme.title: Dream Kitchens and Bathrooms with Mark Millar
programme.desc: The DIY expert helps homeowners transform the most important rooms in the house. This time, Mark helps create a dream country cottage bathroom for £2,000 in a Leicestershire village for Chris and Celie, and an all-black-and-gold kitchen for £25,000 in Hove, Sussex

programme.title: Red 2 (2013)
programme.desc: Former CIA agent Frank Moses reunites his squad of retired operatives for a vital mission. A devastatingly powerful new weapon has been stolen, and it falls to Frank's team to retrieve it - which means facing lethal assassins, terrorists and corrupt government officials out to steal the device. Action thriller sequel, starring Bruce Willis, Helen Mirren and John Malkovich

programme.title: The Disappearance of Shannon Matthews
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary about the disappearance of nine-year-old Shannon Matthews in Yorkshire in 2008, an investigation that gripped the nation, which turned out to be hoax kidnapping plot devised by her mother. Featuring exclusive interviews with Shannon's best friend, members of the local community, and the police officers who eventually found her, the programme reveals the remarkable truth of what really happened

programme.title: The Clues That Caught the Killer
programme.desc: Documentary examining the vital breakthroughs that helped police catch the killers of 60-year-old Sadie Hartley, who was immobilised with a stun gun and stabbed to death in her own home in January 2016. Detectives quickly identified two suspects, but only patient and thorough police work could uncover the evidence to secure a conviction

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: A profile of Californian Edmund Kemper, who murdered his grandparents as a teenager and, upon release from a secure hospital after five years, went on to kill six college girls, his mother and her best friend. The programme examines whether his mind was twisted by his upbringing in a broken home and volatile relationship with a domineering mother

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
